name: Moong Dal Shorba
preparation: Wash and soak moong dal for 30 minutes.
Pressure cook dal with turmeric and water for 3-4 whistles.
Heat ghee in a pan, add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add garlic, ginger, onion and sauté until golden.
Add tomato and cook until soft.
Add cooked dal, mix well and simmer for 5 minutes.
Blend partially for a soup-like consistency.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve warm.
notes: A light, protein-rich dinner perfect for easy digestion.

name: Masoor Dal Light
preparation: Wash masoor dal and pressure cook with turmeric and water for 2-3 whistles.
Heat ghee in a pan, add cumin seeds and dry red chilli.
Add garlic, onion and sauté until translucent.
Add chopped tomato and cook until soft.
Pour cooked dal into the tempering, mix well.
Simmer for 5 minutes on low heat.
Garnish with coriander and serve with roti.
notes: Masoor dal cooks quickly and is very light for dinner.

name: Palak Dal
preparation: Wash toor dal and pressure cook with turmeric for 3-4 whistles.
Blanch spinach in boiling water for 2 minutes, drain and puree.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add garlic, onion and sauté until golden.
Add tomato and red chilli powder, cook until soft.
Add cooked dal and spinach puree, mix well.
Simmer for 8-10 minutes on low heat.
Garnish with coriander and serve with chapati.
notes: Iron-rich spinach combined with protein-rich dal makes a nutritious dinner.

name: Lasooni Dal
preparation: Wash toor dal and pressure cook with turmeric for 3-4 whistles.
Heat ghee in a pan, add cumin seeds and dry red chillies.
Add crushed garlic and sauté until golden and fragrant.
Add onion and cook until translucent.
Add tomato and red chilli powder, cook until mushy.
Pour cooked dal into the tempering, mix well.
Simmer for 10 minutes on low heat.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot with rice or roti.
notes: Garlic gives this dal a bold, aromatic flavor.

name: Mixed Dal Tadka
preparation: Wash all dals together and pressure cook with turmeric for 4 whistles.
Mash the cooked dals lightly with a spoon.
Heat ghee in a pan, add cumin seeds, dry red chillies.
Add garlic, ginger and sauté until fragrant.
Add onion, cook until golden brown.
Add tomato and red chilli powder, cook until soft.
Pour the tadka over the cooked dal, mix well.
Simmer for 5-7 minutes and garnish with coriander.
notes: Combining multiple dals gives a richer taste and nutrition profile.

name: Chana Dal Tadka
preparation: Soak chana dal for 1 hour, wash and drain.
Pressure cook with turmeric and water for 4-5 whistles.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add garlic, ginger, onion and sauté until golden.
Add tomato and red chilli powder, cook until soft.
Add cooked chana dal, mix well.
Simmer for 10 minutes on low heat.
Garnish with coriander and serve with roti.
notes: Chana dal is high in protein and has a nutty flavor.

name: Toor Dal Light
preparation: Wash toor dal and pressure cook with turmeric for 3 whistles.
Mash the dal to a smooth consistency.
Heat ghee, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, curry leaves and dry red chilli.
Add asafoetida and tomato, cook until soft.
Pour the cooked dal into the tempering.
Add salt and simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve warm with steamed rice or roti.
notes: A simple, everyday dal that's easy to digest at night.

name: Sambhar Rice
preparation: Wash toor dal and pressure cook with turmeric for 3 whistles.
Boil drumstick, bottle gourd and onion until tender.
Extract tamarind juice and add to the vegetables.
Add sambhar powder, salt and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add cooked dal and mix well. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, dry red chilli and asafoetida.
Pour tempering into the sambhar.
Garnish with coriander and serve with idli, dosa or rice.
notes: A South Indian staple, perfect as a light dinner.

name: Khichdi Light
preparation: Wash rice and moong dal together 2-3 times.
Heat ghee in a pressure cooker, add cumin seeds.
Add ginger and onion, sauté for 2 minutes.
Add rice, dal, turmeric, salt and water.
Pressure cook for 3 whistles on medium heat.
Let pressure release naturally.
Mash lightly with a spoon for a soft consistency.
Garnish with coriander and serve with pickle or papad.
notes: The ultimate comfort dinner - light and easy to digest.

name: Bajra Khichdi Bowl
preparation: Wash bajra and moong dal together.
Heat ghee in a pressure cooker, add cumin seeds and ajwain.
Add bajra, dal, turmeric, salt and water.
Pressure cook for 4-5 whistles on medium heat.
Let pressure release naturally.
Stir well - the khichdi should be porridge-like.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve hot with a dollop of ghee and kadhi or buttermilk.
notes: Traditional Rajasthani winter dinner, very warming.

name: Moong Khichdi Light
preparation: Wash rice and moong dal together until water runs clear.
Heat ghee in a pressure cooker, add cumin seeds.
Add ginger and sauté for 30 seconds.
Add rice, dal, turmeric, pepper, salt and water.
Pressure cook for 2-3 whistles.
Let pressure release naturally.
The consistency should be soft and semi-liquid.
Serve with lemon wedge and papad.
notes: Perfect when you want something extremely light for dinner.

name: Masala Khichdi Bowl
ingredients: 1/8 cup mixed vegetables (peas, carrots, beans), diced
preparation: Wash rice and dal together.
Heat ghee in a pressure cooker, add cumin seeds.
Add onion and sauté until golden.
Add tomato and all spice powders, cook for 2 minutes.
Add mixed vegetables and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add rice, dal, salt and water.
Pressure cook for 3 whistles.
Garnish with coriander and serve with raita.
notes: A flavorful khichdi with vegetables for added nutrition.

name: Creamy Palak Paneer
preparation: Blanch spinach in boiling water for 2 minutes, transfer to ice water.
Blend spinach into a smooth puree.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add garlic, ginger, green chillies and sauté for 1 minute.
Add onion and cook until golden.
Add tomatoes and cook until they break down.
Add spinach puree, garam masala and salt. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add paneer cubes, mix gently and cook for 3 minutes.
Add cream if using, serve with roti.
notes: A classic North Indian dinner dish, rich in iron and protein.

name: Paneer Tikka
preparation: Mix curd, tikka masala, red chilli powder, turmeric, lemon juice and salt.
Add paneer cubes, capsicum and onion to the marinade.
Marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Thread paneer and vegetables onto skewers.
Grill on a hot tawa or in the oven at 200°C for 12-15 minutes.
Turn occasionally until charred spots appear.
Sprinkle chaat masala and serve hot.
Serve with mint chutney and salad.
notes: Light yet satisfying dinner with smoky flavors.

name: Paneer Bhurji Light
preparation: Crumble paneer into small pieces.
Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds.
Add onion and sauté until translucent.
Add green chilli and capsicum, cook for 2 minutes.
Add tomato and turmeric, cook until tomato is soft.
Add crumbled paneer and salt, mix well.
Cook for 3-4 minutes on medium heat.
Garnish with coriander and serve with roti or paratha.
notes: Quick 15-minute dinner option, high in protein.

name: Kadai Paneer Light
ingredients: 1/2 tbsp kadai masala (coriander + dry red chilli, crushed)
preparation: Heat oil in a kadai/wok, add cumin seeds.
Add garlic, ginger and sauté for 30 seconds.
Add onion and cook until slightly soft.
Add tomato puree and cook for 5-7 minutes until oil separates.
Add kadai masala and salt, mix well.
Add capsicum and cook for 2 minutes.
Add paneer cubes and toss gently.
Cook for 3-4 minutes, garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Use freshly pounded kadai masala for authentic flavor.

name: Aloo Gobhi Light
preparation: Heat oil, add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add onion and sauté until translucent.
Add turmeric, red chilli powder and tomato.
Cook until tomato is soft.
Add potatoes and cauliflower, mix well.
Cover and cook on low heat for 15-18 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add garam masala in the last 2 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve with roti.
notes: A dry preparation that's lighter for dinner.

name: Mix Veg Light
preparation: Heat oil, add cumin seeds.
Add onion and sauté until golden.
Add tomato puree, turmeric and red chilli powder.
Cook until oil separates.
Add all vegetables and salt, mix well.
Add 1/4 cup water, cover and cook for 12-15 minutes.
Add garam masala and mix.
Garnish with coriander and serve with chapati.
notes: Use seasonal vegetables for best taste.

name: Lauki Sabzi
ingredients: 1 cup bottle gourd (lauki), peeled and diced
preparation: Heat oil, add cumin seeds.
Add onion and sauté until translucent.
Add green chilli and tomato, cook for 2 minutes.
Add diced lauki and turmeric powder.
Add salt, cover and cook on low heat for 15-18 minutes.
Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Cook until lauki is soft and translucent.
Garnish with coriander and serve with roti.
notes: One of the lightest vegetables for dinner, aids digestion.

name: Turai Sabzi
ingredients: 1 1/2 medium turai (ridge gourd), peeled and sliced
preparation: Peel turai and cut into round slices.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds.
Add onion and sauté until translucent.
Add tomato, turmeric and red chilli powder.
Cook until tomato is soft.
Add turai slices and salt, mix well.
Cover and cook on low heat for 10-12 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve with roti.
notes: Turai is very low-calorie, excellent for weight management.

name: Smoky Baingan Bharta
preparation: Roast whole baingan directly over flame until skin is charred.
Let it cool, peel off the charred skin.
Mash the roasted baingan roughly.
Heat mustard oil, add cumin seeds.
Add garlic, onion and sauté until golden.
Add green chilli, tomatoes and red chilli powder.
Cook until tomatoes are mushy.
Add mashed baingan, mix well and cook for 5-7 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve with roti.
notes: Smoky flavor from direct roasting makes this dish special.

name: Bhindi Fry Light
preparation: Wash bhindi and pat completely dry before cutting.
Heat oil in a pan on medium-high heat.
Add bhindi and spread in a single layer.
Let it cook undisturbed for 3-4 minutes to reduce sliminess.
Add onion and stir gently.
Add all spice powders and salt.
Cook on medium heat for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add amchur, toss and serve with roti.
notes: Keep bhindi completely dry to avoid sticky texture.

name: Karela Fry
preparation: Slice karela thin, sprinkle salt and set aside for 20 minutes.
Squeeze out the bitter juice and rinse.
Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add karela slices and spread them out.
Cook for 5 minutes without stirring to let them crisp.
Add onion, turmeric and red chilli powder.
Stir-fry for 8-10 minutes until karela is crispy.
Sprinkle amchur powder and serve with roti and dal.
notes: Salting and squeezing reduces bitterness significantly.

name: Methi Aloo
ingredients: 1 cup fresh methi (fenugreek) leaves, washed and chopped
preparation: Wash methi leaves thoroughly, chop and sprinkle salt to reduce bitterness.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds.
Add onion and sauté until translucent.
Add potatoes, turmeric and red chilli powder.
Cover and cook for 10 minutes on low heat.
Add methi leaves and mix well.
Cook uncovered for 5-7 minutes until methi wilts.
Serve with roti or paratha.
notes: Methi is excellent for digestion and blood sugar control.

name: Tandoori Chicken
ingredients: Lemon wedges and onion rings for serving
preparation: Make deep cuts in the chicken pieces.
Mix curd, ginger-garlic paste, tandoori masala, red chilli powder, turmeric, lemon juice, oil and salt.
Coat chicken thoroughly with the marinade.
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours (overnight for best results).
Preheat oven to 220°C or prepare a grill.
Place chicken on a wire rack and cook for 25-30 minutes.
Turn halfway through for even cooking.
Sprinkle chaat masala and serve with onion rings and lemon wedges.
notes: Marinate overnight for the most flavorful tandoori chicken.

name: Chicken Tikka
preparation: Mix curd, ginger-garlic paste, tikka masala, red chilli powder, turmeric, lemon juice and salt.
Add chicken cubes and marinate for at least 1 hour.
Thread chicken onto skewers.
Grill on high heat or in oven at 220°C for 12-15 minutes.
Turn skewers halfway for even charring.
Baste with oil if drying out.
Sprinkle chaat masala.
Serve hot with mint chutney and salad.
notes: High-protein, low-carb dinner option.

name: Grilled Chicken
preparation: Flatten chicken breasts slightly with a mallet.
Mix curd, ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, red chilli powder, cumin powder, lemon juice and salt.
Marinate chicken for 1-2 hours.
Heat a grill pan or tawa with a little oil.
Place chicken and grill for 5-6 minutes per side.
Ensure internal temperature reaches 75°C.
Let rest for 3 minutes before slicing.
Serve with salad and mint chutney.
notes: One of the leanest high-protein dinner options.

name: Chicken Seekh Kebab
preparation: Mix chicken mince with onion, green chillies, ginger-garlic paste, all spices, coriander, mint and egg.
Knead the mixture well for 5 minutes until sticky.
Refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Shape mixture onto skewers in cylindrical form.
Grill on a hot tawa or over charcoal for 4-5 minutes per side.
Baste lightly with oil while grilling.
Cook until golden brown and cooked through.
Serve with mint chutney and onion rings.
notes: Refrigerating the mixture helps kebabs hold shape on skewers.

name: Malai Tikka
ingredients: 1 tsp cream cheese or processed cheese, grated
preparation: Mix cream, curd, cream cheese, ginger-garlic paste, white pepper, cardamom powder, lemon juice and salt.
Add chicken cubes and marinate for 2 hours.
Thread onto skewers.
Grill in oven at 200°C for 12-15 minutes.
Turn halfway and baste with oil.
Cook until golden with slight char marks.
The tikka should be creamy white, not red.
Serve immediately with green chutney.
notes: A milder, creamier alternative to regular tikka.

name: Hariyali Tikka
preparation: Blend coriander, mint, green chillies into a fine paste.
Mix green paste with curd, ginger-garlic paste, cumin powder, lemon juice and salt.
Add chicken cubes and marinate for 2 hours.
Thread onto skewers with capsicum and onion pieces.
Grill on high heat or in oven at 220°C for 12-15 minutes.
Turn halfway for even cooking.
The chicken should have a green coating with slight char.
Serve with mint chutney and onion rings.
notes: The green herb marinade gives a fresh, vibrant flavor.

name: Achari Chicken
preparation: Heat mustard oil until it smokes, then reduce heat.
Add fenugreek, fennel, nigella and mustard seeds.
Let them splutter.
Add onion and sauté until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato puree, red chilli powder and turmeric.
Cook until oil separates.
Add chicken pieces, salt and 1/2 cup water.
Cover and cook for 20-25 minutes until chicken is done.
Serve with roti or rice.
notes: Pickle-style spices give this dish a tangy, bold flavor.

name: Lemon Chicken
preparation: Marinate chicken with lemon juice, ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, pepper and salt for 30 minutes.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add green chilli and sauté briefly.
Add marinated chicken and cook on high heat for 2 minutes.
Reduce heat, cover and cook for 12-15 minutes.
Uncover and cook on high to evaporate excess liquid.
The chicken should be slightly caramelized.
Garnish with coriander and serve with rice.
notes: A tangy, South Indian-inspired chicken dish.

name: Fish Tikka
ingredients: 130g firm fish fillet (surmai or rawas), cubed
preparation: Pat fish cubes dry with paper towel.
Mix curd, ginger-garlic paste, tikka masala, red chilli powder, turmeric, lemon juice and salt.
Gently coat fish cubes in marinade. Refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Thread onto skewers carefully.
Grill on a hot tawa or in oven at 200°C for 8-10 minutes.
Turn once carefully - fish is delicate.
Sprinkle chaat masala.
Serve with mint chutney and lemon wedges.
notes: Don't over-marinate fish, 30 minutes is enough.

name: Tandoori Fish
preparation: Make diagonal cuts on both sides of the fish.
Mix curd, ginger-garlic paste, tandoori masala, red chilli powder, turmeric, lemon juice, oil and salt.
Apply marinade inside cuts and all over the fish.
Refrigerate for 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 200°C.
Place fish on a wire rack over a baking tray.
Bake for 15-18 minutes, turning once.
Serve with onion rings, lemon wedges and green chutney.
notes: Mustard oil gives the fish an authentic tandoori flavor.

name: Fish Fry South
ingredients: 1 tbsp coconut oil for shallow frying
preparation: Wash fish pieces and pat dry.
Mix red chilli powder, turmeric, ginger-garlic paste, curry leaves, lemon juice and salt.
Apply marinade generously on both sides.
Let it marinate for 30 minutes.
Heat coconut oil in a pan on medium heat.
Place fish pieces and fry for 4-5 minutes per side.
Cook until golden brown and crispy.
Serve hot with lemon wedges and onion salad.
notes: Coconut oil gives the authentic South Indian fish fry flavor.

name: Pomfret Fry Light
preparation: Make diagonal slits on both sides of the pomfret.
Mix red chilli powder, turmeric, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, pepper and salt.
Rub marinade into the slits and all over.
Let it rest for 20-30 minutes.
Heat oil in a non-stick pan on medium heat.
Place pomfret and cook for 5-6 minutes per side.
Cook until skin is crispy and golden.
Serve with lemon wedges and green chutney.
notes: Use minimal oil in a non-stick pan for a lighter version.

name: Paneer Tikka Skewers
preparation: Mix curd, tikka masala, red chilli powder, lemon juice and salt.
Add paneer, capsicum, onion and tomatoes to the marinade.
Marinate for 30 minutes.
Thread alternately onto skewers.
Grill on a hot tawa or in oven at 200°C for 10-12 minutes.
Turn skewers for even cooking.
Brush with a little oil while grilling.
Serve hot with mint chutney.
notes: Colorful presentation makes this great for entertaining.

name: Soya Chaap Tikka
preparation: Squeeze excess water from soya chaap.
Mix curd, tikka masala, red chilli powder, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice and salt.
Marinate soya chaap for 1 hour.
Heat oil in a grill pan or tawa.
Place soya chaap and grill for 3-4 minutes per side.
Press with a spatula for char marks.
Sprinkle chaat masala.
Serve with mint chutney and onion rings.
notes: High-protein vegetarian alternative to chicken tikka.

name: Mushroom Tikka
preparation: Wipe mushrooms clean with a damp cloth (don't wash).
Mix curd, tikka masala, red chilli powder, turmeric, lemon juice and salt.
Add mushrooms and marinate for 30 minutes.
Thread onto skewers with capsicum and onion.
Grill on tawa or in oven at 200°C for 8-10 minutes.
Turn for even cooking.
Mushrooms should be slightly charred and juicy inside.
Sprinkle chaat masala and serve hot.
notes: Don't wash mushrooms - they absorb water. Just wipe clean.

name: Veg Seekh Kebab
ingredients: 1/4 cup mixed vegetables (carrots, beans, peas), boiled and finely chopped
preparation: Mix mashed potatoes, chopped vegetables, paneer, green chilli, ginger, garam masala, coriander and salt.
Add breadcrumbs to bind the mixture.
Shape into cylindrical kebabs on skewers.
Refrigerate for 15 minutes to firm up.
Brush with oil and grill on tawa or in oven.
Cook for 3-4 minutes per side until golden.
Alternatively, shallow fry in a non-stick pan.
Serve with green chutney and onion salad.
notes: Can be made ahead and refrigerated until grilling.

name: Dal Tadka
preparation: Wash toor dal and pressure cook with turmeric for 3-4 whistles.
Mash dal to desired consistency.
Add tomato and salt to the dal, simmer for 5 minutes.
For tadka: heat ghee in a small pan.
Add cumin seeds, dry red chillies, garlic slices and ginger.
Sauté until garlic is golden.
Add onion and cook until brown.
Add red chilli powder, pour entire tadka over dal.
Garnish with coriander and serve with rice or roti.
notes: The sizzling tadka added at the end is what makes this dish special.

name: Dal Fry
preparation: Wash both dals and pressure cook with turmeric for 3-4 whistles.
Heat butter in a pan, add cumin seeds.
Add garlic, ginger and green chilli, sauté for 1 minute.
Add onion and cook until golden brown.
Add tomatoes and red chilli powder, cook until mushy.
Add cooked dal and salt, mix well.
Simmer on low for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with coriander and serve with jeera rice or roti.
notes: Restaurant-style dal fry, richer than plain dal.

name: Yellow Dal Simple
preparation: Wash moong dal and pressure cook with turmeric for 2-3 whistles.
Mash dal until smooth.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and asafoetida.
Add tomato and cook until soft.
Pour dal into the tempering, add salt.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve with rice or roti.
notes: The simplest dal - quick, nutritious and easily digestible.

name: Spinach Moong Dal
preparation: Wash moong dal and pressure cook with turmeric for 2-3 whistles.
Add chopped spinach to the hot dal, it will wilt quickly.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and dry red chilli.
Add garlic and sauté until golden.
Add tomato and cook until soft.
Pour dal-spinach mixture into the tempering.
Add salt and simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve with roti.
notes: Iron-rich spinach with protein-rich dal - nutritious combination.

name: Anda Curry
preparation: Boil eggs for 10 minutes, peel and make small slits.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds.
Add onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste, cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato puree, turmeric and red chilli powder.
Cook until oil separates from the masala.
Add 1 cup water and salt, bring to a boil.
Add eggs and simmer for 10 minutes.
Sprinkle garam masala and coriander, serve with rice or roti.
notes: Slitting the eggs allows the gravy to seep in for better flavor.

name: Masala Egg Bhurji
preparation: Beat eggs lightly with a fork.
Heat butter/oil in a pan, add onion.
Sauté until translucent.
Add green chilli and tomato, cook for 2 minutes.
Add turmeric and red chilli powder.
Pour beaten eggs into the pan.
Stir continuously with a spatula to scramble.
Cook until eggs are set but still moist.
Garnish with coriander, serve with pav or roti.
notes: Quick 10-minute dinner, Indian-style scrambled eggs.

name: Boiled Egg Masala
preparation: Boil eggs for 10 minutes, peel and halve.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds and curry leaves.
Add onion and sauté until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste, cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato puree, turmeric and red chilli powder.
Cook until masala thickens and oil separates.
Place halved eggs in the masala, cut side up.
Spoon masala over the eggs, simmer for 5 minutes.
Garnish with coriander, serve with rice.
notes: Semi-dry egg preparation, great with plain rice.

name: Egg Pepper Fry
preparation: Boil eggs, peel and halve.
Heat coconut oil, add fennel seeds and curry leaves.
Add onion and sauté until golden.
Add turmeric, red chilli powder and crushed pepper.
Mix well and cook for 1 minute.
Add halved eggs, gently toss to coat with masala.
Cook for 3-4 minutes on medium heat.
Serve hot as a side dish with rice and rasam.
notes: A South Indian egg preparation with bold pepper flavor.

name: Roti Sabzi Light
preparation: Knead wheat flour with water and salt into soft dough. Rest for 15 minutes.
For sabzi: heat oil, add cumin seeds.
Add tomato and spices, cook until soft.
Add vegetables and 2 tbsp water, cover and cook for 10-12 minutes.
Divide dough into 4 balls, roll into thin rotis.
Cook on hot tawa, puff on direct flame.
Apply a little ghee on hot rotis.
Serve rotis with the vegetable sabzi.
notes: Classic everyday Indian dinner combination.

name: Chapati Dal Dinner Plate
preparation: Knead flour with water and salt into soft dough, rest for 15 minutes.
Wash dal and pressure cook with turmeric for 3 whistles.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds, onion and tomato.
Add cooked dal, salt and simmer for 5 minutes.
Roll dough into 4 thin chapatis.
Cook on hot tawa until golden spots appear.
Arrange chapatis with dal, pickle and salad.
Garnish dal with coriander and serve.
notes: A balanced dinner plate with carbs, protein and fiber.

name: Missi Roti
preparation: Mix wheat flour, besan, onion, green chillies, coriander, ajwain, red chilli powder and salt.
Add water gradually and knead into a semi-stiff dough.
Divide into 4 portions and roll into thick rotis.
Cook on a hot tawa, press with a cloth to cook evenly.
Apply a little oil or ghee on both sides.
Cook until golden brown spots appear.
Serve with curd, pickle and butter.
notes: Besan adds protein and a nutty flavor to the roti.

name: Jowar Roti Sabzi
preparation: Knead jowar flour with hot water and salt into soft dough.
Divide into 4 balls, flatten on a plastic sheet using palms.
Cook on a hot tawa on medium heat until cooked through.
Apply ghee on hot rotis.
For sabzi: heat oil, add cumin seeds, tomato and spices.
Add vegetables, cover and cook for 12-15 minutes.
Serve jowar rotis hot with the sabzi.
notes: Jowar roti is gluten-free and great for digestive health.

name: Bajra Roti with Sabzi
ingredients: Jaggery and white butter for serving (optional)
preparation: Mix bajra flour with salt.
Add hot water gradually and knead into soft dough.
Divide into 4 balls, flatten on a plastic sheet using palms.
Cook on a hot tawa on low-medium heat.
Flip and cook until light brown spots appear.
Apply generous ghee on hot rotis.
Serve with garlic chutney, jaggery and white butter.
notes: Traditional Rajasthani/Gujarati winter dinner roti.

name: Ragi Roti
preparation: Mix ragi flour with onion, coriander, green chilli, curry leaves and salt.
Add hot water and knead into soft dough.
Divide into 4 portions, flatten on a wet plastic sheet.
Cook on a hot tawa on medium heat.
Flip when bottom is cooked.
Apply a little oil on both sides.
Cook until both sides are firm and slightly crispy.
Serve with coconut chutney and sambhar.
notes: Ragi is rich in calcium and iron, excellent for dinner.

name: Tomato Pepper Rasam
preparation: Cook tomatoes in 2 cups water until soft, mash them.
Extract tamarind juice and add to tomatoes.
Add rasam powder, turmeric and salt.
Add cooked toor dal water (thin consistency).
Bring to a boil, then simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Heat ghee, add mustard seeds, cumin, garlic, curry leaves and asafoetida.
Pour tempering into the rasam.
Garnish with coriander. Serve with steamed rice.
notes: A comforting South Indian soup, great for cold evenings.

name: Mixed Veg Shorba
preparation: Boil all vegetables in water until tender (10-12 minutes).
In a separate pan, sauté garlic, ginger and onion.
Add tomato and cook until soft.
Add boiled vegetables with their water.
Season with pepper and salt.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve hot as a light dinner soup.
notes: One of the lightest dinner options, great for weight loss.

name: Tomato Shorba
preparation: Cook tomatoes, onion, garlic, ginger, peppercorns, cloves and bay leaf in 2 cups water for 15 minutes.
Remove bay leaf and whole spices.
Blend the mixture until smooth.
Strain through a sieve for a silky texture.
Return to pan, add salt and sugar.
Heat butter and add to the shorba.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: A Mughlai-style tomato soup, rich and velvety.

name: Palak Ka Shorba
preparation: Blanch spinach in boiling water for 2 minutes, drain.
Sauté onion, garlic and ginger in butter until soft.
Add blanched spinach and water.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Blend until smooth.
Return to pan, add nutmeg, pepper, salt.
Simmer for 3 minutes.
Drizzle cream if using and serve hot.
notes: Iron-rich, low-calorie dinner soup.

name: Moong Dal Cheela
preparation: Drain soaked moong dal and blend with green chilli, ginger and a little water into a smooth batter.
Add salt, onion and coriander to the batter.
Heat a non-stick tawa, spread a little oil.
Pour a ladleful of batter and spread into a thin crepe.
Cook on medium heat until bottom is golden.
Flip and cook the other side.
Make 4 chillas from the batter.
Serve with green chutney and curd.
notes: High-protein, gluten-free dinner option.

name: Besan Cheela
preparation: Mix besan with turmeric, red chilli powder, ajwain and salt.
Add water gradually to make a smooth, thin batter.
Add onion, green chilli and coriander.
Heat a non-stick tawa, spread a little oil.
Pour a ladleful and spread thin.
Cook until bottom is golden, flip and cook other side.
Make 4 chillas.
Serve with green chutney and ketchup.
notes: Quick 15-minute dinner, rich in protein.

name: Oats Cheela
preparation: Powder oats in a blender.
Mix oat powder with besan, turmeric and salt.
Add water to make a medium-thin batter.
Add onion, carrot, green chilli and coriander.
Heat non-stick tawa, spread oil.
Pour batter and spread thin.
Cook until golden on both sides.
Serve with mint chutney.
notes: Oats add fiber for better satiety.

name: Tawa Sabzi
ingredients: 1/2 cup mixed vegetables (capsicum, onion, tomato, mushroom), sliced
preparation: Heat butter on a flat tawa/griddle.
Add sliced onions and cook for 2 minutes.
Add capsicum and mushrooms, stir-fry on high heat.
Add turmeric, red chilli powder and pav bhaji masala.
Add tomatoes and salt.
Toss everything together on high heat for 3-4 minutes.
Squeeze lemon juice and garnish with coriander.
Serve with roti or pav.
notes: High-heat tawa cooking keeps vegetables crunchy.

name: Kadai Vegetable
ingredients: 1/2 cup mixed vegetables (capsicum, carrots, beans, peas)
1/2 tbsp kadai masala (crushed coriander + dry red chillies)
preparation: Heat oil in a kadai, add cumin seeds.
Add onion and sauté until golden.
Add tomato puree and cook until oil separates.
Add kadai masala and salt.
Add vegetables and 2 tbsp water.
Cook on medium heat for 8-10 minutes.
Vegetables should be cooked but still have a bite.
Top with ginger julienne and coriander, serve with roti.
notes: Use freshly pounded kadai masala for best results.

name: Dry Aloo Fry
preparation: Parboil potato cubes for 5 minutes, drain.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds.
Add potato cubes and spread in a single layer.
Cook undisturbed for 3-4 minutes to get crispy bottom.
Stir and repeat for even crispiness.
Add turmeric, red chilli powder and salt.
Cook for another 5 minutes, tossing occasionally.
Add amchur powder, garnish with coriander. Serve with roti and dal.
notes: Parboiling before frying gives the best crispy texture.

name: Gobi Tawa Fry
preparation: Blanch cauliflower florets in boiling water for 3 minutes, drain.
Heat oil on a flat tawa/pan.
Add onion and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add blanched cauliflower, spread in a single layer.
Let it cook undisturbed for 3-4 minutes for browning.
Flip and add all spice powders and salt.
Toss well and cook for 5-6 more minutes.
Garnish with coriander, serve with roti.
notes: Blanching first ensures gobi cooks through while staying crispy outside.

name: Chicken Curry Light
preparation: Heat oil, add cumin seeds.
Add onions and cook until dark golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste, cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato puree, turmeric and red chilli powder.
Cook until oil separates from masala.
Add chicken pieces and salt, coat well.
Add 1 cup water, cover and cook for 20-25 minutes.
Sprinkle garam masala and coriander, serve with roti or rice.
notes: Use bone-in chicken for richer flavor in the gravy.

name: Kadhi
preparation: Whisk curd and besan together until smooth.
Add turmeric, red chilli powder, salt and water. Mix well.
Pour into a pan and bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
Reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
For tempering: heat oil, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds.
Add curry leaves, dry red chilli and asafoetida.
Pour tempering into the kadhi.
Serve hot with steamed rice.
notes: Use slightly sour curd for authentic kadhi taste.

name: Rajasthani Kadhi Pakora
preparation: Whisk curd, besan, turmeric, red chilli powder and salt with water.
Bring to a boil while stirring continuously.
Simmer on low for 20-25 minutes until thick.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, cumin, fenugreek seeds.
Add curry leaves, dry chillies and asafoetida.
Pour tempering into kadhi.
The kadhi should be thicker than Punjabi kadhi.
Serve with steamed rice or bajra roti.
notes: Rajasthani kadhi is thicker and tangier than Punjabi version.

name: Sprout Chaat Salad
preparation: Steam or boil sprouts for 5-7 minutes until slightly tender.
Let them cool to room temperature.
Add chopped onion, tomato, cucumber and green chilli.
Add lemon juice, chaat masala and salt.
Mix everything gently.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve immediately as a light dinner.
notes: High-protein, low-fat dinner perfect for weight loss.

name: Chana Chaat
preparation: Boil or pressure cook chana until tender but not mushy.
Drain and let cool slightly.
Add onion, tomato, green chilli and coriander.
Add lemon juice, chaat masala, cumin powder and salt.
Toss everything together.
Top with sev for crunch.
Serve as a protein-rich light dinner.
notes: A tangy, protein-packed dinner option.

name: Moong Salad
preparation: Soak green moong overnight, boil until tender.
Drain and let cool.
Add onion, tomato, cucumber, green chilli.
Add lemon juice, chaat masala and salt.
Mix gently.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve at room temperature or slightly chilled.
notes: An extremely light yet protein-rich dinner.

name: Thayir Sadam
preparation: Mash cooked rice slightly with a spoon.
Add curd and milk, mix well.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and urad dal.
When dal turns golden, add curry leaves, green chilli and ginger.
Add asafoetida.
Pour tempering over the curd rice.
Add salt and mix well.
Serve at room temperature or chilled with pickle.
notes: A cooling, probiotic-rich South Indian dinner favorite.

name: Khichdi Kadhi
preparation: Wash rice and dal, pressure cook with turmeric, salt, ghee and water for 3 whistles.
For kadhi: whisk curd, besan and water until smooth.
Bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Simmer 10 minutes.
Temper with mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Mash khichdi lightly.
Serve khichdi topped with kadhi.
A classic comfort dinner combo.
notes: The most comforting Indian dinner combo - perfect when unwell.

name: Dalia Khichdi
preparation: Wash dalia and moong dal together.
Heat ghee in a pressure cooker, add cumin seeds.
Add vegetables and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add dalia, dal, turmeric, salt and water.
Pressure cook for 3-4 whistles.
Let pressure release naturally.
Stir well - should have a porridge-like consistency.
Serve hot with pickle or curd.
notes: Dalia is high in fiber, keeps you full longer.

name: Paneer Kachumber
preparation: Cut paneer into small cubes.
Optionally, lightly pan-fry paneer for golden color.
Combine cucumber, tomato, capsicum and pomegranate.
Add paneer cubes.
Dress with lemon juice, chaat masala, pepper and salt.
Toss gently.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve immediately as a light dinner.
notes: A fresh, protein-rich salad dinner.

name: Grilled Paneer
preparation: Mix curd, tandoori masala, red chilli powder, lemon juice and salt.
Marinate paneer slices for 30 minutes.
Heat a grill pan with a little oil.
Place paneer slices and grill for 2-3 minutes per side.
Press gently for char marks.
Serve on a bed of salad greens.
Drizzle with mint chutney.
notes: A low-carb, high-protein dinner.

name: Paneer Steak
preparation: Mix curd, garam masala, pepper, cumin, lemon juice and salt.
Marinate paneer slabs for 30-45 minutes.
Heat a cast iron pan with oil on high heat.
Place paneer slabs and sear for 3-4 minutes per side.
Press down with a spatula for even browning.
The paneer should have a crusty exterior.
Serve with grilled vegetables.
Drizzle with any remaining marinade.
notes: Thick paneer slabs work best - at least 1 inch thick.

name: Desi Veg Soup
preparation: Heat butter, sauté garlic and ginger.
Add all vegetables and stir for 2 minutes.
Add water, salt and bring to a boil.
Simmer for 15 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Blend half the soup for a thicker consistency (optional).
Add pepper and adjust salt.
Serve hot with whole wheat bread.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
notes: Partially blending gives a nice thick-yet-chunky texture.

name: Clear Veg Soup
preparation: Bring stock/water to a boil.
Add garlic, ginger and soy sauce.
Add carrots first (they take longest to cook).
After 3 minutes, add cabbage and mushrooms.
Simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Add spring onion greens and pepper.
Adjust salt.
Serve piping hot.
notes: Extremely low-calorie, ideal dinner for weight management.

name: Mulligatawny Light
preparation: Cook toor dal until soft.
Heat oil, sauté onion until golden.
Add garlic, ginger, curry leaves.
Add carrot and tomato, cook for 3 minutes.
Add curry powder, turmeric and salt.
Add cooked dal and 2 cups water.
Simmer for 15 minutes.
Add coconut milk, simmer 5 more minutes.
Squeeze lemon juice and serve.
notes: An Anglo-Indian soup that's warm and hearty.

name: Sweet Corn Soup
ingredients: 1/2 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp water
preparation: Blend half the corn with 1 cup water until smooth.
Heat butter, sauté garlic and onion.
Add whole corn kernels and cook for 2 minutes.
Add corn puree and remaining water.
Bring to a boil and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add cornstarch mixture to thicken, stir well.
Season with pepper and salt.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: Half blended, half whole corn gives the best texture.

name: Hot Sour Soup
preparation: Bring stock to a boil.
Add carrots, cabbage and mushrooms.
Cook for 5 minutes.
Add soy sauce, vinegar and chilli sauce.
Add tofu cubes gently.
Mix cornstarch with 2 tbsp water, add to soup while stirring.
Cook for 2-3 minutes until slightly thick.
Season with pepper and salt. Garnish with spring onion.
notes: A spicy, tangy Indo-Chinese soup.

name: Dosa
ingredients: 1/2 cup dosa batter (rice + urad dal, fermented)
preparation: Heat a non-stick tawa or cast iron dosa pan.
Sprinkle a few drops of water and wipe clean.
Pour a ladleful of batter and spread in circular motion.
Drizzle oil around the edges.
Cook on medium heat until bottom is golden and crispy.
Place potato masala filling in the center.
Fold and serve immediately.
Serve with coconut chutney and sambhar.
notes: Dosa batter needs to be fermented overnight for best results.

name: Vegetable Uttapam
preparation: Heat a non-stick tawa on medium heat.
Pour batter and spread thick (not thin like dosa).
Immediately scatter onion, tomato, coriander and green chilli on top.
Press toppings gently into the batter.
Drizzle oil around edges.
Cover and cook on low-medium for 3-4 minutes.
Flip carefully and cook other side for 2 minutes.
Serve with coconut chutney and sambhar.
notes: Uttapam is thicker than dosa, like a savory pancake.

name: Idli
ingredients: 1/2 cup idli batter (rice + urad dal, fermented)
preparation: Grease idli moulds with a little oil.
Pour batter into each mould, filling 3/4 full.
Steam for 10-12 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.
Let cool for 1 minute before removing from moulds.
Serve hot with coconut chutney, sambhar.
Optionally serve with idli podi mixed with sesame oil.
Makes about 8 idlis.
notes: Steamed idlis are very light and easy to digest for dinner.

name: Pesarattu
ingredients: 1/2 cup green moong dal, soaked 6 hours
preparation: Drain soaked moong dal, blend with green chillies, ginger and minimal water into smooth batter.
Add salt and mix.
Heat a non-stick tawa.
Pour batter and spread thin like a dosa.
Sprinkle chopped onion on top.
Drizzle oil around edges.
Cook until bottom is golden and crispy.
Serve with ginger chutney (allam pachadi) and upma.
notes: A protein-rich Andhra speciality, no fermentation needed.

name: Appam Stew
ingredients: For appam: 1 cup rice flour, 1/4 cup coconut milk, 1 tsp yeast, salt
For stew: 1 cup mixed vegetables (potato, carrot, peas, beans)
preparation: For appam: mix rice flour, coconut milk, yeast, salt. Ferment 4-6 hours.
Swirl batter in an appam pan to make bowl-shaped crepes.
For stew: heat coconut oil, add cloves, cinnamon, curry leaves.
Add ginger and green chillies.
Add vegetables with thin coconut milk, cook until tender.
Add thick coconut milk, salt. Simmer 5 minutes.
Don't boil after adding thick coconut milk.
Serve appam with the creamy stew.
notes: A Kerala dinner classic - the lacy appam soaks up the coconut stew.

name: Puttu Kadala
ingredients: For puttu: 1 cup rice flour, grated coconut, salt, water
For kadala curry: 1 cup black chickpeas (kala chana), soaked overnight
preparation: For puttu: sprinkle water on rice flour, mix until crumbly. Layer with coconut in puttu maker. Steam 10 minutes.
For kadala: pressure cook soaked chickpeas until tender.
Heat coconut oil, add curry leaves, onion.
Sauté until golden, add tomato and spices.
Add cooked chickpeas and some water.
Simmer for 15 minutes until gravy thickens.
Crumble puttu onto a plate.
Serve with kadala curry on the side.
notes: Iconic Kerala breakfast/dinner combo.

name: Idiyappam
ingredients: Coconut milk or egg curry for serving
preparation: Add salt to rice flour.
Pour hot water and mix quickly with a spoon.
Knead into soft, smooth dough when cool enough to handle.
Fill dough into an idiyappam press.
Press onto greased idli plates in circular motion.
Steam for 8-10 minutes.
The noodle nests should be soft and fluffy.
Serve with coconut milk, egg curry or vegetable stew.
notes: Also called string hoppers, very light for dinner.

name: Prawn Fry Light
preparation: Clean and devein prawns, wash and pat dry.
Marinate with red chilli powder, turmeric, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice and salt.
Let it rest for 15 minutes.
Heat coconut oil in a pan.
Add curry leaves, then marinated prawns.
Spread in a single layer.
Cook for 2-3 minutes per side on medium-high heat.
Serve hot with lemon wedges and onion salad.
notes: Don't overcook prawns - they become rubbery.

name: Crab Soup
preparation: Heat stock, add garlic, ginger and onion.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add crab meat and soy sauce.
Mix cornstarch with water, add to soup while stirring.
Cook for 3 minutes until slightly thick.
Slowly drizzle beaten egg while stirring for egg ribbons.
Season with pepper and salt.
Garnish with spring onion and serve hot.
notes: A protein-rich, comforting seafood soup.

name: Fish Moilee Light
preparation: Marinate fish with turmeric and salt for 15 minutes.
Heat coconut oil, add onion and curry leaves.
Sauté until onion is soft.
Add ginger and green chillies.
Add thin coconut milk and bring to a simmer.
Gently place fish pieces in the gravy.
Cook for 8-10 minutes on low heat.
Add thick coconut milk, simmer 3 minutes (don't boil).
Serve with appam or rice.
notes: A mild Kerala fish curry in coconut milk.

name: Keema Light
preparation: Heat oil, add onion and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste, cook for 1 minute.
Add mince and cook on high heat, breaking lumps.
Cook until mince changes color.
Add tomatoes, turmeric and red chilli powder.
Cook until tomatoes are mushy.
Add peas and 1/4 cup water, cover and cook for 15 minutes.
Add garam masala, garnish with coriander. Serve with roti.
notes: Cook mince on high heat initially to get a good sear.

name: Seekh Kebab
preparation: Mix mince with onion, green chillies, ginger-garlic paste, all spices, herbs and besan.
Knead well for 5-7 minutes until sticky.
Refrigerate for 1 hour.
Shape onto skewers in cylindrical form.
Grill over charcoal or in oven at 220°C.
Cook for 5-6 minutes per side.
Baste with oil while grilling.
Serve with mint chutney, onion rings and roomali roti.
notes: Roasted besan acts as a binder and prevents kebabs from falling apart.

name: Mutton Shorba
preparation: Add mutton bones, onion, garlic, ginger and all whole spices to a pressure cooker.
Add water and salt.
Pressure cook for 5-6 whistles until meat is fall-off-bone tender.
Strain the broth, pressing solids to extract flavor.
Return clear broth to pan and bring to a simmer.
Adjust salt and add pepper.
Add lemon juice.
Serve hot, garnished with coriander. Optionally add shredded meat.
notes: A nourishing bone broth - great for immunity and joint health.

name: Veg Biryani Light
ingredients: 1/3 cup mixed vegetables (potato, carrot, peas, beans, cauliflower)
Few saffron strands in warm milk
preparation: Parboil rice in salted water until 70% done, drain.
Fry sliced onions until golden brown, set aside.
In same pan, add ginger-garlic paste, vegetables, curd, spices and salt. Cook 5 minutes.
Layer: vegetables at bottom, then rice, then fried onions, mint, coriander, saffron milk.
Seal with foil/lid. Cook on very low heat (dum) for 20 minutes.
Let it rest 5 minutes before opening.
Gently mix layers while serving.
Serve with raita.
notes: Dum cooking on low heat gives biryani its signature aroma.

name: Pulao Light
preparation: Wash and soak basmati rice for 20 minutes, then drain.
Heat ghee in a pot, add cloves, bay leaf, cinnamon, and cardamom.
Add sliced onion and sauté until light golden.
Add carrot, peas, and beans; sauté for 2 minutes.
Add drained rice and gently stir for a minute.
Pour in water, add salt, and bring to a boil.
Cover tightly and cook on low heat for 12-15 minutes.
Fluff with a fork, garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A fragrant, light rice dish with mixed vegetables for an easy dinner.

name: Jeera Rice Dal
preparation: Wash and soak rice for 15 minutes; drain.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds; when they splutter, add rice.
Add water, salt, and cook on low heat for 12-15 minutes.
Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric and water for 3-4 whistles.
For tadka: heat oil, add mustard seeds, dry red chillies, and curry leaves.
Add chopped tomato, cook until soft.
Pour tadka into cooked dal and simmer for 5 minutes.
Serve jeera rice with hot dal on the side.
notes: Classic Indian comfort dinner of cumin-flavored rice paired with toor dal.

name: Palak Rice
ingredients: 1 cup fresh spinach, blanched and puréed
preparation: Wash and soak rice for 15 minutes, then drain.
Blanch spinach in boiling water, cool and blend into a purée.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and let them crackle.
Add garlic, onion, and green chilli; sauté until golden.
Add spinach purée and cook for 2 minutes.
Add rice and water, season with salt.
Cover and cook on low heat for 12-15 minutes.
Fluff gently and serve with raita.
notes: Iron-rich spinach rice, a nutritious green dinner option.

name: Chitranna
preparation: Cook rice and let it cool slightly; spread on a plate.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, and chana dal.
When dal turns golden, add dry red chillies and curry leaves.
Add turmeric and roasted peanuts; stir well.
Pour the tempering over the rice.
Add lemon juice and salt, mix gently without breaking grains.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
notes: Tangy South Indian lemon rice, a quick and flavorful dinner.

name: Thengai Sadam
preparation: Cook rice and spread on a plate to cool slightly.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and urad dal; let them splutter.
Add dry red chillies, curry leaves, and cashews.
Add grated coconut and sauté for 2-3 minutes on low heat.
Add the cooked rice, salt, and mix gently.
Serve warm with pickle or curry on the side.
notes: Fragrant coconut rice from South India, simple and satisfying.

name: Aloo Tikki Dinner Light
preparation: Mash boiled potatoes while warm in a bowl.
Add besan, cumin powder, garam masala, chilli powder, green chilli, coriander, and salt.
Mix well and shape into flat round tikkis.
Heat oil on a tawa/griddle on medium heat.
Shallow fry tikkis until golden and crispy on both sides.
Drain on paper towel and serve with green chutney and salad.
notes: Crispy potato tikkis make a satisfying light dinner with chutney.

name: Corn Cutlet
preparation: Roughly crush half the corn kernels, keep rest whole.
Mix corn with mashed potato, cornflour, green chilli, coriander, cumin, and salt.
Form into flat round tikkis.
Heat oil on a tawa and shallow fry tikkis until golden on both sides.
Serve hot with mint chutney and onion rings.
notes: Sweet corn tikkis - a fun and tasty light dinner option.

name: Beetroot Cutlet
preparation: Squeeze excess water from grated beetroot.
Combine beetroot, mashed potato, rice flour, cumin, chaat masala, green chilli, and salt.
Mix well and shape into flat tikkis.
Heat oil on a tawa, shallow fry tikkis on medium heat until crispy on both sides.
Serve with yogurt dip or green chutney.
notes: Vibrant beetroot tikkis packed with nutrients for a colorful dinner.

name: Methi Paratha Light
preparation: Wash and finely chop methi leaves; remove stems.
Combine flour, methi leaves, ajwain, chilli powder, turmeric, salt, and oil.
Knead into a soft dough with water; rest for 15 minutes.
Divide into portions, roll each into a flat paratha.
Cook on a hot tawa with minimal oil until golden on both sides.
Serve with curd or pickle.
notes: Methi parathas are iron-rich and great for a wholesome dinner.

name: Stuffed Palak Paratha
preparation: Blanch spinach, cool, and blend into a smooth purée.
Mix flour, spinach purée, green chilli, cumin powder, salt, and oil.
Knead into a soft green dough; rest for 15 minutes.
Divide into balls and roll into flat parathas.
Cook on hot tawa with minimal oil until golden on both sides.
Serve with raita or achaar.
notes: Spinach parathas are a nutritious way to get greens into dinner.

name: Stuffed Mooli Paratha
preparation: Grate radish and squeeze out excess water.
Mix flour, grated mooli, green chilli, ajwain, chilli powder, and salt.
Knead into a soft dough using water as needed; rest for 10 minutes.
Divide into portions, roll into parathas.
Cook on a hot tawa with a little oil until golden and crispy on both sides.
Serve with butter, curd, or pickle.
notes: Classic Punjabi mooli paratha, a flavorful and filling dinner.

name: Vegetable Stew Kerala
preparation: Boil potato, carrot, peas, and beans until just tender; drain.
Heat coconut oil, add cloves, cinnamon, and curry leaves.
Add sliced onion, ginger, and green chillies; sauté lightly.
Add boiled vegetables and coconut milk.
Simmer on low heat for 8-10 minutes; do not boil vigorously.
Season with salt and serve with appam or rice.
notes: Creamy Kerala vegetable stew, perfect with appam or bread.

name: Aviyal
preparation: Cook drumstick, raw banana, yam, beans, and carrot with turmeric and salt until just tender.
Grind coconut, cumin, and green chillies into a coarse paste.
Add the coconut paste to the vegetables and mix gently.
Simmer on low heat for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat, stir in curd and curry leaves.
Drizzle coconut oil on top and serve with rice.
notes: A traditional Kerala mixed vegetable dish with coconut and curd.

name: Kerala Thoran
preparation: Heat coconut oil, add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
Add curry leaves, onion, and green chillies; sauté for 2 minutes.
Add shredded cabbage and turmeric; mix well.
Sprinkle water, cover and cook on low for 5-7 minutes until cabbage is tender.
Add grated coconut, stir, and cook for 2 more minutes.
Serve as a side with rice and sambar.
notes: Kerala-style dry coconut stir-fry with cabbage, light and nutritious.

name: Sundal
ingredients: 1/2 cup black chickpeas (kala chana), soaked overnight
preparation: Pressure cook soaked chickpeas with salt until tender; drain.
Heat coconut oil, add mustard seeds and urad dal.
When they splutter, add red chillies, curry leaves, and asafoetida.
Add boiled chickpeas and sauté for 3-4 minutes.
Add grated coconut, toss well.
Serve warm as a protein-rich dinner snack.
notes: High-protein South Indian sundal, a temple-style healthy dish.

name: Mixed Veg Kootu
preparation: Cook chana dal and pumpkin together with turmeric until soft.
Grind coconut, cumin, and green chillies into a smooth paste.
Add coconut paste to the dal-pumpkin mixture.
Simmer for 5-7 minutes until everything blends together.
For tempering: heat coconut oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Pour tempering over the kootu and serve with rice.
notes: South Indian kootu with dal and pumpkin in coconut gravy.

name: Poriyal Stir Fry
preparation: Heat coconut oil, add mustard seeds and urad dal; let them splutter.
Add dry red chillies and curry leaves.
Add chopped onion and sauté until translucent.
Add chopped beans and turmeric; sprinkle water.
Cover and cook on medium heat for 8-10 minutes until beans are tender.
Add grated coconut, stir well, and serve with rice and sambar.
notes: Tamil Nadu style beans poriyal, a classic everyday dinner side.

name: Sambar Idli
preparation: Cook toor dal with turmeric until soft; mash lightly.
Soak tamarind in warm water and extract pulp.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, and asafoetida.
Add onion, sauté until golden. Add tomato and cook until soft.
Add drumstick pieces, sambar powder, and tamarind pulp.
Add cooked dal and enough water; simmer for 15 minutes.
Cut idlis into halves and soak in hot sambar for 5 minutes.
Serve sambar idli garnished with coriander.
notes: Soft idlis dunked in sambar make a comforting South Indian dinner.

name: Rasam with Vada
ingredients: 1/2 cup urad dal, soaked for 3 hours
preparation: Grind soaked urad dal into a thick batter; add onion, green chillies, and salt.
Shape into vadas and deep fry until golden and crispy.
For rasam: extract tamarind pulp; cook tomatoes until soft.
Add rasam powder, tamarind pulp, and water; boil for 10 minutes.
Temper with mustard seeds, cumin, curry leaves, and asafoetida.
Soak vadas briefly in warm water and place in serving bowl.
Pour hot rasam over vadas, garnish with coriander.
notes: Crispy vadas in tangy rasam, a classic South Indian dinner combination.

name: Curd Vada
preparation: Grind soaked urad dal into a fluffy batter; add salt.
Shape into round vadas and deep fry until golden brown.
Soak fried vadas in warm water for 15 minutes; gently squeeze out water.
Whisk curd with cumin powder, chilli powder, and salt.
Place soaked vadas in a dish, pour curd mixture generously over them.
Drizzle tamarind chutney, sprinkle chaat masala and coriander.
Refrigerate for 30 minutes and serve chilled.
notes: Soft curd vadas are a cooling and protein-rich light dinner.

name: Chilla Roll
preparation: Mix besan with water to make a smooth, thin batter.
Add onion, tomato, green chilli, turmeric, ajwain, coriander, and salt.
Heat a non-stick tawa, grease lightly with oil.
Pour a ladleful of batter and spread into a thin circle.
Cook until edges lift, flip and cook the other side.
Spread green chutney on the chilla and roll tightly.
Serve hot with ketchup or chutney.
notes: Protein-packed besan chilla rolls for a quick, tasty dinner.

name: Moong Dal Wrap
ingredients: Shredded lettuce, cucumber, and chutney for filling
preparation: Grind soaked moong dal into a thick, spreadable batter.
Add cumin seeds, salt, and a little water if needed.
Heat a non-stick tawa, grease with oil.
Spread batter into a thin round; cook until bottom is golden.
Flip and cook the other side.
Place lettuce, cucumber, onion, coriander, and chutney on the wrap.
Roll tightly and serve.
notes: Crispy moong dal wraps with fresh veggies, a healthy dinner option.

name: Besan Roll
preparation: Make a smooth batter with besan, water, cumin, chilli powder, and salt.
Heat tawa, grease lightly; spread batter into a thin crepe.
Cook until set, flip and cook other side.
Mix crumbled paneer, capsicum, and onion for the filling.
Place filling on the besan crepe, drizzle mint chutney.
Roll tightly and cut in half to serve.
notes: Besan rolls stuffed with paneer, a protein-rich dinner.

name: Cauliflower Rice
ingredients: 1/2 medium cauliflower, grated into rice-sized pieces
1/4 cup mixed vegetables (peas, carrot, beans), diced
preparation: Grate cauliflower into rice-sized granules using a food processor or grater.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds and let them crackle.
Add onion and green chilli; sauté until soft.
Add mixed vegetables and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add cauliflower rice, turmeric, garam masala, and salt.
Stir-fry on high heat for 5-7 minutes until tender but not mushy.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Low-carb cauliflower rice Indian style, great for weight-conscious dinners.

name: Cauliflower Fried Rice
ingredients: 1/2 medium cauliflower, grated into rice-sized pieces
preparation: Grate cauliflower into rice-like granules.
Heat oil in a wok on high heat; add garlic and sauté.
Add onion, carrot, cabbage, and capsicum; stir-fry for 3 minutes.
Add cauliflower rice and toss well on high heat.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, and black pepper.
Stir-fry for 5-6 minutes until cauliflower is cooked but firm.
Garnish with spring onion and serve hot.
notes: Indo-Chinese style fried rice using cauliflower, very low calorie.

name: Broccoli Sabzi
preparation: Blanch broccoli florets in boiling water for 2 minutes; drain.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds; when they crackle, add garlic and onion.
Sauté until golden, add tomato and cook until soft.
Add turmeric, chilli powder, and coriander powder.
Add blanched broccoli and toss well.
Cook covered for 3-4 minutes on medium heat.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve with roti.
notes: Simple Indian-style broccoli sabzi, nutritious and low calorie.

name: Zucchini Sabzi
preparation: Heat oil, add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add chopped onion and sauté until soft.
Add tomato and cook until mushy.
Add turmeric, coriander powder, and chilli powder; mix well.
Add cubed zucchini, salt, and stir to coat with masala.
Cover and cook on medium heat for 8-10 minutes until zucchini is tender.
Garnish with coriander and serve with roti or rice.
notes: Light zucchini sabzi with Indian spices, low calorie dinner side.

name: Bell Pepper Paneer Stir Fry
preparation: Cut paneer and all bell peppers into even cubes.
Heat oil in a wok on high heat; add garlic.
Add paneer cubes and sear until golden on edges; remove.
In the same wok, add onion and bell peppers; stir-fry for 3-4 minutes.
Add soy sauce, black pepper, chilli flakes, and salt.
Return paneer to the wok, toss everything together.
Serve hot as a dinner side or with rice.
notes: Colorful bell pepper paneer stir-fry, high protein and vibrant.

name: Baby Corn Stir Fry
preparation: Blanch baby corn in boiling water for 2 minutes; drain.
Heat oil, add garlic and onion; sauté on high heat.
Add capsicum and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add baby corn, cumin, coriander, and chilli powder.
Add soy sauce and salt; toss well on high heat for 3 minutes.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve.
notes: Quick baby corn stir-fry with Indian-Chinese flavors.

name: Soya Chunk Curry Light
ingredients: 1/2 cup soya chunks, soaked in hot water
preparation: Soak soya chunks in hot water for 15 minutes; squeeze out water.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds; when they crackle add onion purée.
Cook until onion turns golden brown.
Add tomato purée and cook until oil separates.
Add turmeric, coriander, chilli powder, and salt.
Add soya chunks and 1/2 cup water; simmer for 10 minutes.
Add garam masala, garnish with coriander, serve with roti.
notes: High-protein soya chunk curry, a budget-friendly dinner option.

name: Soya Keema
preparation: Soak soya granules in hot water for 10 minutes; squeeze out water.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds, then garlic and ginger.
Add chopped onion and sauté until golden.
Add tomato, coriander powder, and chilli powder; cook until tomato is soft.
Add soya granules and green peas; mix well.
Add 1/4 cup water, cover and cook for 10 minutes.
Sprinkle garam masala, garnish with coriander, and serve with roti.
notes: Soya keema mimics minced meat texture - high protein vegetarian dinner.

name: Soya Biryani Light
ingredients: 1/2 cup soya chunks, soaked and squeezed
Pinch of saffron in 2 tbsp warm milk
preparation: Soak rice for 20 minutes; parboil until 70% done, drain.
Soak soya chunks in hot water, squeeze out water.
Heat oil, add whole spices; add onion and fry until golden.
Add soya chunks, tomato, curd, and biryani masala; cook for 5 minutes.
Layer soya mixture and parboiled rice in a pot.
Drizzle saffron milk, add mint and coriander.
Cover tightly and cook on dum (very low heat) for 20 minutes.
Gently mix and serve with raita.
notes: Soya biryani - a protein-packed vegetarian version of classic biryani.

name: Tofu Bhurji
preparation: Crumble tofu with hands to resemble scrambled texture.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds; when they crackle add onion and green chilli.
Sauté until onion is golden.
Add tomato, turmeric, and chilli powder; cook until tomato is soft.
Add crumbled tofu, salt, and mix well.
Cook on medium heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala and coriander; serve with roti or bread.
notes: Tofu bhurji is a vegan alternative to paneer bhurji, equally delicious.

name: Tofu Tikka
ingredients: 1/4 cup thick curd (or cashew cream for vegan)
Onion rings and lemon wedges for serving
preparation: Cut tofu into cubes and press gently to remove excess water.
Mix curd, besan, tikka masala, chilli powder, turmeric, lemon juice, oil, and salt.
Marinate tofu cubes in this mixture for 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 200°C or use a grill pan.
Thread tofu on skewers or place on a baking tray.
Grill/bake for 15-20 minutes, turning once, until charred spots appear.
Serve with onion rings, lemon wedges, and mint chutney.
notes: Tandoori-style tofu tikka, a great plant-based protein dinner.

name: Tofu Curry
preparation: Lightly pan-fry tofu cubes until golden; set aside.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds; add onion purée and cook until golden.
Add tomato purée, turmeric, coriander, and chilli powder.
Cook until oil separates.
Add coconut milk and 1/2 cup water; bring to a simmer.
Add fried tofu cubes and cook for 8-10 minutes.
Sprinkle garam masala, garnish with coriander, and serve with rice.
notes: Creamy Indian-style tofu curry with coconut milk.

name: Palak Soup
preparation: Heat butter, add cumin seeds; when they crackle add garlic and onion.
Sauté until onion is soft.
Add potato cubes and water; cook until potato is tender.
Add spinach and cook for 3-4 minutes until wilted.
Cool slightly and blend into a smooth soup.
Return to pot, season with salt and black pepper.
Reheat and serve with a squeeze of lemon.
notes: Creamy spinach soup with Indian spices, light and nourishing.

name: Bottle Gourd Soup
ingredients: 1 cup bottle gourd (lauki), peeled and cubed
preparation: Heat butter, add garlic, ginger, and onion; sauté until soft.
Add bottle gourd cubes and water.
Cook until lauki is completely soft, about 15 minutes.
Cool slightly and blend into a smooth soup.
Return to pot, add cumin powder, black pepper, and salt.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve warm.
notes: Very low-calorie bottle gourd soup, excellent for weight loss dinners.

name: Carrot Ginger Soup
preparation: Heat butter, add cumin seeds; when they crackle add garlic, ginger, and onion.
Sauté for 3 minutes until fragrant.
Add carrots, turmeric, and water.
Cook until carrots are very soft, about 15 minutes.
Blend into a smooth, creamy soup.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Serve warm with a drizzle of cream if desired.
notes: Warming carrot ginger soup with Indian spices, great for immunity.

name: Masala Corn
preparation: Boil corn kernels until tender; drain.
Heat butter in a pan, add corn kernels.
Sprinkle chaat masala, chilli powder, black pepper, and salt.
Toss well on medium heat for 2-3 minutes.
Squeeze lemon juice and mix.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot.
notes: Tangy masala corn, a popular street-food style light dinner.

name: Corn Chaat Bowl
preparation: In a bowl, combine boiled corn kernels, onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice, chaat masala, cumin powder, and salt.
Toss well to mix all the flavors.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve immediately at room temperature or slightly warm.
notes: Fresh and tangy corn chaat, a light and refreshing dinner.

name: Corn Cups
preparation: Boil corn kernels until tender; drain well.
Melt butter in a pan, add corn kernels.
Sprinkle black pepper, dried herbs, garlic powder, and salt.
Sauté on medium heat for 3-4 minutes.
Serve in cups, topped with chilli flakes.
notes: Butter herb corn cups, a cozy and simple dinner snack.

name: Desi Chicken Shorba
ingredients: Fresh coriander and lemon juice for serving
preparation: Heat ghee, add garlic and ginger; sauté until fragrant.
Add onion and carrot; cook for 3 minutes.
Add chicken breast pieces, turmeric, and water.
Bring to a boil, then simmer for 20 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Shred chicken with two forks; return to the soup.
Season with cumin, black pepper, and salt.
Serve hot with lemon juice and coriander on top.
notes: Warming Indian chicken soup, high-protein and healing.

name: Mutton Soup
ingredients: Fresh coriander and lemon for serving
preparation: Wash mutton bones thoroughly.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds, cloves, and cinnamon.
Add onion, garlic, and ginger; sauté until golden.
Add mutton bones and sear for 3-4 minutes.
Add water and bring to a boil; skim off froth.
Simmer on low heat for 45-60 minutes until meat falls off bones.
Strain the soup, season with pepper and salt.
Serve hot with lemon and coriander.
notes: Traditional mutton bone soup, rich in collagen and extremely flavorful.

name: Bone Broth
preparation: Wash bones thoroughly and place in a large pot.
Add all other ingredients and water.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat to lowest setting.
Simmer for 3-4 hours, skimming foam occasionally.
Strain through a fine mesh sieve.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve warm or store for later use.
notes: Slow-simmered bone broth rich in collagen and minerals.

name: Mixed Salad Plate
preparation: Arrange cucumber, tomato, onion rings, and grated carrot on a plate.
Top with boiled chickpeas.
Sprinkle green chilli, chaat masala, and cumin powder.
Squeeze lemon juice and add salt.
Toss gently and garnish with coriander.
Serve immediately as a light dinner plate.
notes: A refreshing Indian salad plate with chickpeas for extra protein.

name: Raita Plate
preparation: Whisk curd until smooth in a bowl.
Squeeze excess water from grated cucumber.
Add cucumber, onion, tomato, and mint to the curd.
Season with cumin powder, black salt, red chilli powder, and salt.
Mix well and chill for 10 minutes.
Serve raita with warm chapatis for a light dinner.
notes: Cool raita with chapati makes a refreshing, probiotic-rich dinner.

name: Sprout Bowl
preparation: Steam mixed sprouts for 8-10 minutes until tender.
In a bowl, combine steamed sprouts, onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice, chaat masala, cumin powder, and salt.
Toss well to combine all flavors.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve at room temperature or slightly warm.
notes: Protein-packed sprout bowl, one of the healthiest light dinners.

name: Veg Clear Soup Rice
preparation: Boil vegetable stock with garlic, ginger, and onion.
Add carrot, beans, and cabbage; cook for 5 minutes.
Add soy sauce, black pepper, and salt.
Place cooked rice in a serving bowl.
Pour hot clear soup over the rice.
Garnish with spring onion and serve immediately.
notes: Light clear soup served over rice, gentle on the stomach for dinner.

name: Millet Khichdi
preparation: Wash millet and moong dal together; soak for 15 minutes.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds.
Add onion and sauté until soft.
Add tomato and cook for 2 minutes.
Add millet, dal, turmeric, salt, and water.
Pressure cook for 3 whistles or simmer covered for 20 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve with pickle or curd.
notes: Gluten-free millet khichdi, a wholesome and easily digestible dinner.

name: Foxtail Millet
preparation: Dry roast foxtail millet for 2-3 minutes; set aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and urad dal; let them splutter.
Add curry leaves, green chillies, and onion; sauté until soft.
Add mixed vegetables and cook for 2 minutes.
Add roasted millet, turmeric, salt, and water.
Cover and cook on low heat for 15-18 minutes until millet is fluffy.
Fluff with a fork and serve with chutney or sambar.
notes: Foxtail millet upma-style dinner, high fiber and gluten-free.

name: Little Millet
preparation: Wash little millet and soak for 10 minutes; drain.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and urad dal; let them splutter.
Add curry leaves, green chillies, and onion; sauté.
Add mixed vegetables and cook for 2 minutes.
Add millet, turmeric, salt, and water.
Cover and cook on low heat for 15 minutes.
Fluff with a fork and serve with sambar or chutney.
notes: Little millet is nutrient-dense and perfect for a gluten-free dinner.

name: Barnyard Millet
preparation: Wash barnyard millet and moong dal together.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds; when they crackle add onion.
Sauté until golden, add tomato and cook for 2 minutes.
Add millet, dal, turmeric, salt, and water.
Pressure cook for 2-3 whistles.
Let pressure release naturally; mash slightly.
Garnish with coriander and serve with pickle.
notes: Barnyard millet khichdi, great for diabetics and weight watchers.

name: Kodo Millet
preparation: Cook kodo millet in water until fluffy; spread on plate to cool.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, and peanuts.
When dal turns golden, add curry leaves, green chillies, and onion.
Sauté until onion is soft.
Add cooked millet and turmeric; toss well.
Squeeze lemon juice, add salt, and mix gently.
Serve as a lemon millet rice.
notes: Kodo millet lemon rice, a tangy and wholesome dinner.

name: Oats Khichdi
preparation: Wash moong dal and soak for 15 minutes.
Heat ghee, add cumin seeds; add onion and sauté.
Add tomato and vegetables; cook for 3 minutes.
Add oats, moong dal, turmeric, salt, and water.
Cook covered on low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
The consistency should be porridge-like but not too runny.
Serve with a squeeze of lemon.
notes: Oats khichdi is fiber-rich and great for a light, healthy dinner.

name: Quinoa Pulao
preparation: Wash quinoa thoroughly and drain.
Heat ghee, add whole spices and cumin seeds.
Add onion and sauté until light golden.
Add mixed vegetables and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add quinoa, turmeric, salt, and water.
Bring to a boil, then cover and cook on low for 15 minutes.
Fluff with a fork, garnish with coriander, and serve.
notes: Quinoa pulao is a complete protein dinner with all essential amino acids.

name: Amaranth Kheer
preparation: Dry roast amaranth grains for 2 minutes on low heat.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Simmer on low heat for 15-20 minutes until grains are soft and porridge-like.
Add milk, ghee, and cardamom powder.
Stir in jaggery or honey for sweetness.
Top with chopped nuts and serve warm.
notes: Amaranth porridge is gluten-free and rich in protein and iron.

name: Stuffed Paratha Light
ingredients: 1/2 cup mixed vegetable filling (potato, peas, cauliflower)
preparation: Knead flour with water and a pinch of salt into soft dough; rest 15 minutes.
For filling: mash boiled potato with peas, cauliflower, cumin, amchur, chilli, and salt.
Divide dough into balls; roll into small circles.
Place filling in center, seal edges, and roll out gently.
Cook on hot tawa with oil until golden on both sides.
Serve with curd and pickle.
notes: Mixed vegetable stuffed paratha, a wholesome Indian dinner favorite.

name: Stuffed Roti
preparation: Knead flour into soft dough with water and salt; rest 15 minutes.
Mix crumbled paneer with green chilli, cumin, garam masala, coriander, and salt.
Divide dough into balls; roll into small circles.
Place paneer filling in center, fold and roll out gently.
Cook on hot tawa with ghee until golden on both sides.
Serve with mint chutney.
notes: Paneer-stuffed rotis for a protein-rich, filling dinner.

name: Laccha Paratha
preparation: Mix both flours, ajwain, and salt; knead into soft dough with water.
Rest the dough for 20 minutes.
Roll each portion into a thin circle.
Apply ghee, fold into pleats, and roll into a spiral coil.
Flatten gently and roll out into a layered paratha.
Cook on tawa with ghee until golden and flaky on both sides.
Serve with dal or curry.
notes: Flaky, layered laccha paratha for a special dinner treat.

name: Tadka Chawal
preparation: Cook rice and let it cool slightly.
Heat ghee in a pan; add cumin seeds and mustard seeds.
When they splutter, add dry red chillies and curry leaves.
Add turmeric and asafoetida; stir quickly.
Add cooked rice and toss gently to coat with the tadka.
Season with salt and garnish with coriander.
Serve with pickle, papad, and curd.
notes: Simple tadka rice, a comforting everyday dinner staple.

name: Bagara Bhat
preparation: Wash and soak rice for 20 minutes; drain.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds, peanuts, and cashews; fry until golden.
Add dry red chillies, curry leaves, and green chillies.
Add sliced onion and sauté until golden.
Add turmeric, rice, salt, and water.
Cover and cook on low heat until rice is fluffy.
Serve with mirchi ka salan or raita.
notes: Hyderabadi bagara rice, aromatic and pairs well with spicy curries.

name: Brinjal Rice
ingredients: 1/2 tbsp vangi bath powder (or sambar powder)
preparation: Extract tamarind pulp in warm water.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, and peanuts.
Add curry leaves and onion; sauté until golden.
Add brinjal cubes and cook until soft.
Add vangi bath powder, tamarind pulp, and salt.
Cook for 5 minutes until masala blends well.
Mix with cooked rice gently and serve.
notes: Karnataka-style vangi bath, tangy brinjal rice with roasted spices.

name: Bisi Bele Hulianna
preparation: Cook rice and toor dal together with water until very soft.
Cook vegetables separately until tender.
Extract tamarind pulp; add to rice-dal mixture with vegetables.
Add bisi bele bath powder and salt; simmer for 10 minutes.
For tadka: heat ghee, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, and cashews.
Pour tadka over the bath; mix well.
Serve hot with boondi raita and papad.
notes: Bisi bele bath is a one-pot Karnataka classic, hearty and nutritious.

name: Tamarind Puliyodarai
preparation: Heat sesame oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, chana dal, and fenugreek seeds.
When they splutter, add red chillies, curry leaves, peanuts, and asafoetida.
Add tamarind paste and turmeric; cook for 5 minutes until thick.
Season with salt.
Add cooked rice and mix gently until well coated.
Serve at room temperature.
notes: Temple-style tamarind rice from Tamil Nadu, deeply flavorful.

name: Imli Rice
preparation: Heat oil, add mustard seeds and urad dal; let them splutter.
Add red chillies, curry leaves, and peanuts; fry until golden.
Add asafoetida and turmeric; stir.
Add tamarind paste, salt, and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add cooked rice and toss gently to coat evenly.
Let it sit for 5 minutes for flavors to meld.
Serve with papad or chips.
notes: Tangy tamarind rice, a South Indian staple that's quick to prepare.

name: Lemon Rice Dinner Light
preparation: Spread cooked rice on a plate to cool; avoid clumps.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, and chana dal.
When they turn golden, add peanuts, curry leaves, and green chillies.
Add turmeric and asafoetida; stir briefly.
Add rice and toss on low heat for 2 minutes.
Remove from heat; add lemon juice and salt; mix gently.
Serve at room temperature with pickle.
notes: Light lemon rice, quick to make and perfect for a simple dinner.

name: Curd Rice Dinner Light
preparation: Mash cooked rice slightly while warm.
Mix in curd and milk; stir until creamy.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and urad dal.
When they splutter, add green chilli, ginger, curry leaves, and asafoetida.
Pour tadka over the curd rice; mix well.
Add salt and refrigerate until cool.
Garnish with pomegranate seeds and serve.
notes: Cooling curd rice, a South Indian favorite for a soothing dinner.

name: Coconut Rice Dinner Light
preparation: Spread cooked rice on a plate to cool.
Heat coconut oil, add mustard seeds and urad dal.
When they splutter, add red chillies, curry leaves, and cashews.
Fry until cashews are golden; add asafoetida.
Add grated coconut and sauté for 1-2 minutes.
Add rice and salt; toss gently.
Serve with pickle or sambar.
notes: Light coconut rice variation, fragrant and delicious.

name: Mushroom Fried Rice
preparation: Heat oil in a wok on high heat; add garlic.
Add mushrooms and sauté until they release water and turn golden.
Add onion, carrot, and capsicum; stir-fry for 3 minutes.
Add cooked rice and toss on high heat.
Add soy sauce, black pepper, and salt.
Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until rice is heated through.
Garnish with spring onion and serve hot.
notes: Savory mushroom fried rice with Indo-Chinese flavors.

name: Paneer Pulao
preparation: Heat oil in a wok; sear paneer cubes until golden. Remove.
In the same wok, add garlic and onion; stir-fry.
Add carrot, cabbage, and capsicum; toss on high heat for 3 minutes.
Add cold cooked rice; stir-fry for 2-3 minutes.
Add soy sauce, black pepper, and salt.
Return paneer to the wok and toss well.
Garnish with spring onion and serve.
notes: Paneer fried rice, a protein-rich Indo-Chinese dinner dish.

name: Soya Fried Rice
ingredients: 1/4 cup soya chunks, soaked and squeezed
preparation: Soak soya chunks in hot water for 10 minutes; squeeze and chop.
Heat oil in a wok, add garlic; stir-fry.
Add soya chunks and toss until lightly browned.
Add onion, carrot, and cabbage; stir-fry on high heat.
Add rice and toss well.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, black pepper, and salt.
Garnish with spring onion and serve hot.
notes: High-protein soya fried rice with Indo-Chinese flavors.

name: Veg Hakka Noodles
preparation: Boil noodles as per package; drain, toss with a few drops of oil.
Heat oil in a wok on high heat; add garlic.
Add onion and all julienned vegetables; stir-fry for 3-4 minutes.
Add cooked noodles and toss vigorously.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, and black pepper.
Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until noodles are coated.
Garnish with spring onion and serve hot.
notes: Street-style veg hakka noodles, a popular Indo-Chinese dinner.

name: Schezwan Noodles
preparation: Boil noodles, drain, and toss with a little oil.
Heat oil in a wok; add garlic and sauté.
Add all vegetables and stir-fry on high heat for 3 minutes.
Add schezwan sauce, soy sauce, and vinegar; mix well.
Add noodles and toss until evenly coated with sauce.
Cook for 2-3 minutes on high heat.
Garnish with spring onion and serve.
notes: Spicy schezwan noodles, bold Indo-Chinese flavors for dinner.

name: Chow Mein
preparation: Boil noodles until al dente; drain and set aside.
Heat oil in a wok on high heat; add garlic and onion.
Add cabbage, carrot, capsicum, and bean sprouts; stir-fry 3 minutes.
Add noodles and toss well.
Add soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, and salt.
Stir-fry for 3 minutes until noodles are lightly charred.
Serve hot.
notes: Classic chow mein with crispy noodles and crunchy vegetables.

name: Gobi Manchurian
preparation: Make batter with cornflour, maida, salt, pepper, and water.
Dip cauliflower florets in batter and deep fry until golden; drain.
Heat 1 tbsp oil, add garlic and ginger; sauté.
Add onion and capsicum; stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add soy sauce, chilli sauce, and ketchup; mix well.
Add fried cauliflower and toss to coat with sauce.
Garnish with spring onion and serve hot.
notes: Crispy Gobi Manchurian, a beloved Indo-Chinese dinner starter.

name: Grilled Salmon
preparation: Pat salmon fillets dry with paper towels.
Mix olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, pepper, paprika, dill, and salt.
Rub the marinade over salmon; let sit for 15 minutes.
Preheat grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Grill salmon skin-side down for 4-5 minutes.
Flip carefully and grill another 3-4 minutes until cooked through.
Serve with lemon wedges and steamed vegetables.
notes: Omega-3 rich grilled salmon, one of the healthiest dinner proteins.

name: Baked Cod Lemon
preparation: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Place cod fillets in a baking dish.
Mix olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, pepper, and salt.
Pour marinade over the fish.
Bake for 15-18 minutes until fish flakes easily with a fork.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve.
