notes: Light baked cod with lemon, lean protein for a healthy dinner.

name: Pan Seared Tilapia
preparation: Pat tilapia fillets dry; season with garlic powder, paprika, pepper, and salt.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium-high heat.
Place fillets in the pan; cook for 3-4 minutes per side.
Fish is done when it flakes easily and is golden on both sides.
Squeeze lemon juice over the fish.
Garnish with parsley and serve with salad.
notes: Quick pan-seared tilapia, a lean and affordable dinner fish.

name: Steamed Sea Bass
preparation: Place sea bass fillets on a heatproof plate.
Season with salt and drizzle soy sauce.
Top with ginger and garlic slices.
Steam over boiling water for 10-12 minutes until fish is opaque.
Heat sesame oil in a small pan until smoking.
Place julienned green onion on fish and pour hot oil over it.
Drizzle rice vinegar and serve immediately.
notes: Delicate steamed sea bass with Asian aromatics, very light dinner.

name: Lemon Herb Chicken
preparation: Mix lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, rosemary, thyme, pepper, and salt.
Marinate chicken breasts for 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F).
Place chicken in a baking dish; pour remaining marinade over.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Let rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve.
notes: Zesty lemon herb chicken, aromatic and juicy for dinner.

name: Baked Chicken Thighs Light
preparation: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Mix olive oil with garlic powder, paprika, oregano, pepper, and salt.
Rub the spice mixture over chicken thighs.
Place on a baking tray lined with parchment paper.
Bake for 30-35 minutes until internal temp reaches 75°C.
Let rest for 5 minutes.
Serve with lemon wedges and steamed vegetables.
notes: Juicy baked chicken thighs with simple spices for an easy dinner.

name: Chicken Stir Fry
ingredients: 1/4 tsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp water
preparation: Slice chicken thin and season with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a wok on high heat; add chicken and cook until browned. Remove.
In the same wok, add garlic; stir-fry briefly.
Add broccoli, bell pepper, and carrot; stir-fry for 3-4 minutes.
Return chicken to wok; add soy sauce and sesame oil.
Add cornstarch slurry and toss until sauce thickens.
Serve with rice or on its own.
notes: Quick chicken stir-fry packed with vegetables, a balanced dinner.

name: Zucchini Noodles Marinara
preparation: Spiralize zucchini into noodle shapes; pat dry.
Heat olive oil, add garlic and cherry tomatoes; sauté 2 minutes.
Add marinara sauce and chilli flakes; simmer for 5 minutes.
Add zucchini noodles and toss gently for 2-3 minutes.
Do not overcook - zoodles should be slightly firm.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with fresh basil and parmesan if desired.
notes: Ultra-low-carb zucchini noodles with marinara, perfect for weight loss.

name: Spaghetti Squash Bowl
preparation: Cut spaghetti squash in half lengthwise; scoop out seeds.
Brush with olive oil, season with salt and pepper.
Bake cut-side down at 200°C for 35-40 minutes until tender.
Scrape flesh with a fork to create spaghetti-like strands.
Heat marinara sauce with garlic and oregano.
Place squash strands in a bowl; top with sauce.
Garnish with basil and parmesan.
notes: Spaghetti squash is a natural low-carb pasta replacement.

name: Cauliflower Rice Stir Fry
preparation: Grate cauliflower into rice-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a wok; scramble egg if using, set aside.
Add garlic and ginger; stir-fry briefly.
Add onion, carrot, and peas; cook for 3 minutes.
Add cauliflower rice and stir-fry on high heat for 5 minutes.
Add soy sauce, sesame oil, and scrambled egg.
Toss well, garnish with spring onion.
notes: Low-carb cauliflower rice stir-fry, great alternative to regular fried rice.

name: Turkey Meatballs
preparation: Mix ground turkey with breadcrumbs, egg white, garlic, onion, herbs, pepper, and salt.
Shape into small meatballs (about 12).
Heat a non-stick pan with a little oil; brown meatballs on all sides.
Add marinara sauce to the pan.
Cover and simmer for 15-20 minutes until meatballs are cooked through.
Garnish with fresh basil.
Serve with zucchini noodles or salad.
notes: Lean turkey meatballs in marinara, lower fat than traditional beef.

name: Lean Beef Stir Fry
preparation: Slice beef thin against the grain; season with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a wok on high heat; sear beef quickly for 2 minutes. Remove.
In the same wok, add garlic, broccoli, bell pepper, and onion.
Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until veggies are crisp-tender.
Return beef to wok; add soy sauce and oyster sauce.
Add cornstarch slurry; toss until sauce thickens.
Serve with rice or cauliflower rice.
notes: Lean beef stir-fry with vegetables, high protein dinner.

name: Beef Broccoli Light
preparation: Marinate beef slices in 1 tbsp soy sauce for 15 minutes.
Blanch broccoli in boiling water for 2 minutes; drain.
Heat oil in wok; sear beef on high heat until browned. Remove.
Add garlic and ginger; stir-fry briefly.
Add broccoli and remaining soy sauce, oyster sauce.
Return beef; add cornstarch slurry and toss until sauce thickens.
Drizzle sesame oil and serve.
notes: Classic beef and broccoli, lighter version with less oil.

name: Shrimp Scampi Light
preparation: Season shrimp with salt and pepper.
Melt butter in a pan over medium-high heat.
Add garlic and chilli flakes; sauté for 30 seconds.
Add shrimp in a single layer; cook 2 minutes per side.
Add wine/stock and lemon juice; simmer for 2 minutes.
Garnish with parsley.
Serve with a side salad or zucchini noodles.
notes: Light shrimp scampi, quick and elegant dinner option.

name: Garlic Butter Shrimp
preparation: Season shrimp with paprika, salt, and pepper.
Heat olive oil and butter in a pan over medium-high heat.
Add garlic; sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add shrimp; cook 2-3 minutes per side until pink and curled.
Squeeze lemon juice over the shrimp.
Garnish generously with parsley.
Serve immediately.
notes: Garlic butter shrimp, ready in under 10 minutes.

name: Garlic Shrimp Stir Fry
preparation: Season shrimp with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a wok; cook shrimp for 2 minutes per side. Remove.
In the same wok, add garlic; stir-fry briefly.
Add broccoli, bell pepper, and carrot; stir-fry for 3-4 minutes.
Return shrimp; add soy sauce and sesame oil.
Add cornstarch slurry and toss until sauce thickens.
Serve hot with rice or on its own.
notes: Healthy shrimp stir-fry loaded with colorful vegetables.

name: Tofu Stir Fry
preparation: Press tofu to remove excess water; cut into cubes.
Heat oil in a wok; pan-fry tofu until golden on all sides. Remove.
Add garlic; stir-fry briefly.
Add broccoli, bell pepper, and carrot; stir-fry for 3-4 minutes.
Return tofu; add soy sauce and sesame oil.
Add cornstarch slurry and toss until sauce coats everything.
Serve hot with rice or noodles.
notes: Tofu stir-fry with crispy edges and colorful vegetables.

name: Tempeh Stir Fry
preparation: Cut tempeh into cubes; steam for 10 minutes to reduce bitterness.
Heat oil in a wok; fry tempeh until golden and crispy. Remove.
Add garlic and ginger; stir-fry briefly.
Add broccoli and bell pepper; stir-fry for 3-4 minutes.
Return tempeh; add soy sauce, sweet chilli sauce, and sesame oil.
Toss well and cook for 1 minute.
Garnish with sesame seeds and serve.
notes: Fermented tempeh stir-fry, rich in probiotics and protein.

name: Edamame Poke Bowl
preparation: Cook sushi rice and season with rice vinegar.
Boil edamame for 3-4 minutes; drain and shell.
Place rice as the base in a bowl.
Arrange edamame, cucumber, avocado, and carrot on top.
Drizzle with soy sauce and sesame oil.
Sprinkle sesame seeds and nori strips.
Serve at room temperature.
notes: Plant-based poke bowl with edamame, fresh and filling.

name: Greek Chicken Plate
preparation: Marinate chicken with olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Grill or pan-sear chicken for 6-7 minutes per side until cooked through.
Let rest for 5 minutes; slice.
Arrange cucumber, cherry tomatoes, and red onion on a plate.
Place sliced chicken alongside the salad.
Top with crumbled feta cheese.
Serve with pita or on its own.
notes: Mediterranean-style Greek chicken plate, balanced and flavorful.

name: Mediterranean Baked Fish
preparation: Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F).
Place fish fillets in a baking dish.
Scatter cherry tomatoes, olives, capers, and garlic around the fish.
Drizzle with olive oil; season with oregano, salt, and pepper.
Bake for 18-20 minutes until fish flakes easily.
Garnish with fresh basil.
Serve straight from the baking dish.
notes: Mediterranean baked fish with tomatoes and olives, light and elegant.

name: Moroccan Chicken
preparation: Heat olive oil; sear chicken thighs until browned. Remove.
In the same pan, add onion and garlic; sauté until soft.
Add cumin, cinnamon, turmeric, and paprika; cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato and chickpeas; cook for 3 minutes.
Return chicken; add 1/2 cup water.
Cover and simmer for 25-30 minutes until chicken is tender.
Garnish with coriander and serve with couscous or rice.
notes: Moroccan spiced chicken with chickpeas, aromatic and comforting.

name: Thai Green Curry Light
ingredients: 200g mixed vegetables (eggplant, zucchini, bamboo shoots, bell pepper)
1/2 tbsp fish sauce or soy sauce
1/2 tsp palm sugar or brown sugar
preparation: Heat oil in a pan; add green curry paste and fry for 1 minute.
Add half the coconut milk and cook until it starts to separate.
Add vegetables and tofu; stir to coat.
Add remaining coconut milk, fish/soy sauce, sugar, and kaffir lime leaf.
Simmer for 10-12 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Add Thai basil leaves and stir.
Serve with steamed jasmine rice.
notes: Light Thai green curry with vegetables, fragrant and satisfying.

name: Pad Thai Dinner Light
ingredients: 1/2 tbsp fish sauce or soy sauce
Lime wedge and chilli flakes for serving
preparation: Soak rice noodles in warm water for 20 minutes; drain.
Mix tamarind paste, fish/soy sauce, lime juice, and sugar for the sauce.
Heat oil; fry tofu until golden. Remove.
Add garlic, then crack egg and scramble quickly.
Add noodles and sauce; toss until noodles absorb the sauce.
Add tofu and bean sprouts; toss briefly.
Serve with crushed peanuts, lime, and chilli flakes.
notes: Lighter Pad Thai with less oil, classic Thai street food flavors.

name: Tom Yum Soup
ingredients: 1/2 tbsp fish sauce or soy sauce
1/2 tsp chilli paste in oil (nam prik pao)
preparation: Bring stock to a boil; add lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves.
Simmer for 5 minutes to infuse flavors.
Add mushrooms and Thai chillies; cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add chilli paste, fish/soy sauce, and stir.
Remove from heat; add lime juice (do not boil after adding lime).
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve hot as a warming dinner soup.
notes: Thai tom yum soup, spicy and sour with aromatic herbs.

name: Miso Glazed Salmon
preparation: Mix miso paste, mirin, sake/vinegar, sugar, and sesame oil.
Coat salmon fillets with the miso glaze.
Marinate for at least 30 minutes (or overnight in fridge).
Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Place salmon on a parchment-lined tray.
Bake for 12-15 minutes until caramelized on top.
Garnish with sesame seeds and spring onion.
notes: Japanese-style miso salmon, umami-rich and succulent.

name: Teriyaki Tofu
ingredients: Sesame seeds and spring onion for garnish
preparation: Press tofu to remove water; slice into thick steaks.
Mix soy sauce, mirin, honey, rice vinegar, garlic, and ginger for teriyaki sauce.
Heat oil in a pan; pan-fry tofu until golden on both sides.
Pour teriyaki sauce over the tofu.
Add cornstarch slurry to thicken the sauce.
Cook for 2-3 minutes until sauce is glossy and coats the tofu.
Garnish with sesame seeds and spring onion.
notes: Glossy teriyaki tofu, a satisfying plant-based dinner.

name: Japanese Curry Light
preparation: Heat oil; sauté onion until translucent.
Add potato and carrot; cook for 3 minutes.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Simmer for 15 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Break curry roux into pieces; stir into the pot.
Simmer on low for 5 minutes until sauce thickens.
Serve over steamed rice.
notes: Mild Japanese curry with vegetables, comfort food from Japan.

name: Stuffed Zucchini
preparation: Cut zucchini in half lengthwise; scoop out centers to make boats.
Mix quinoa, black beans, corn, tomato, cumin, chilli powder, salt, and pepper.
Fill zucchini boats with the mixture.
Top with shredded cheese.
Bake at 190°C (375°F) for 20-25 minutes until zucchini is tender.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Stuffed zucchini boats with quinoa and beans, healthy and creative.

name: Stuffed Bell Pepper
preparation: Cut tops off bell peppers; remove seeds and membranes.
Mix brown rice, black beans, corn, tomato, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Stuff bell peppers with the filling.
Top with shredded cheese.
Place in a baking dish; bake at 190°C for 25-30 minutes.
Peppers should be tender and cheese melted.
Serve hot.
notes: Colorful stuffed bell peppers, a complete dinner in one package.

name: Eggplant Rollatini Light
preparation: Slice eggplant lengthwise into 1/4-inch thick slices.
Brush with olive oil; grill or bake until pliable.
Mix ricotta, half the mozzarella, parmesan, egg yolk, basil, salt, and pepper.
Spread filling on each eggplant slice; roll up.
Place rolls seam-side down in a baking dish.
Pour marinara sauce over; top with remaining mozzarella.
Bake at 190°C for 20-25 minutes until bubbly.
notes: Light eggplant rollatini with ricotta, Italian-inspired dinner.

name: Chicken Lettuce Wraps
preparation: Heat oil; cook ground chicken until browned, breaking it up.
Add garlic, ginger, and onion; sauté for 2 minutes.
Add water chestnuts; stir.
Add soy sauce, hoisin sauce, and sesame oil; cook 2 minutes.
Wash and separate lettuce leaves into cups.
Spoon chicken mixture into lettuce cups.
Garnish with spring onion and serve.
notes: Low-carb chicken lettuce wraps, fun and flavorful dinner.

name: Lettuce Wrap Tacos
preparation: Season black beans with cumin, chilli powder, and salt; warm in a pan.
Mix corn, tomato, onion, and coriander for a salsa.
Wash and separate lettuce leaves into taco shells.
Layer beans in each lettuce cup.
Top with corn salsa and diced avocado.
Squeeze lime juice over each wrap.
Serve immediately.
notes: Lettuce wrap tacos with beans and avocado, low-carb Mexican style.

name: Asian Lettuce Cups
preparation: Heat oil; add garlic and ginger, sauté briefly.
Add mushrooms and carrot; stir-fry on high heat for 4-5 minutes.
Add water chestnuts; stir.
Add soy sauce, hoisin sauce, and sesame oil; cook 2 minutes.
Wash and arrange lettuce leaves as cups.
Spoon mushroom mixture into each cup.
Top with crispy noodles if desired.
notes: Vegetarian Asian lettuce cups with umami mushroom filling.

name: Grilled Vegetable Plate
preparation: Cut all vegetables into grill-friendly pieces.
Toss with olive oil, Italian herbs, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Preheat grill or grill pan to medium-high.
Grill vegetables for 3-4 minutes per side until charred and tender.
Arrange on a plate.
Drizzle with balsamic glaze.
Serve with hummus or as a side.
notes: Charred grilled vegetables, simple and satisfying dinner.

name: Roasted Root Vegetables
preparation: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Cut all root vegetables into even-sized cubes.
Toss with olive oil, rosemary, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Spread in a single layer on a baking tray.
Roast for 35-40 minutes, tossing halfway, until golden and tender.
Serve warm as a hearty dinner side.
notes: Sweet roasted root vegetables with rosemary, comforting dinner.

name: Sheet Pan Vegetables
preparation: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Spread all vegetables on a large sheet pan.
Drizzle with olive oil; season with garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Toss to coat evenly in a single layer.
Roast for 25-30 minutes until vegetables are tender and edges are crispy.
Garnish with fresh herbs and serve.
notes: Easy sheet pan roasted vegetables, minimal effort dinner.

name: Lentil Bolognese
preparation: Cook lentils in water until tender (about 25 minutes); drain.
Heat olive oil; sauté onion, carrot, celery, and garlic for 5 minutes.
Add crushed tomatoes, Italian herbs, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Simmer for 15 minutes.
Add cooked lentils and stir; simmer for 5 more minutes.
Serve over whole wheat pasta, zucchini noodles, or polenta.
Garnish with fresh basil.
notes: Hearty lentil bolognese, a protein-rich plant-based dinner.

name: Chole Stew
preparation: Heat olive oil; sauté onion and garlic until soft.
Add cumin, paprika, and cinnamon; stir for 1 minute.
Add crushed tomatoes and chickpeas.
Simmer for 15-20 minutes until stew thickens.
Add spinach and stir until wilted.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with coriander and serve with crusty bread.
notes: Spiced chickpea stew with spinach, hearty and plant-based.

name: White Bean Soup
preparation: Heat olive oil; sauté onion, garlic, carrot, and celery for 5 minutes.
Add white beans, vegetable stock, thyme, and bay leaf.
Bring to a boil, then simmer for 20 minutes.
Remove bay leaf; blend half the soup for a creamy texture.
Return to pot and stir.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with parsley and serve with bread.
notes: Creamy white bean soup, comforting and full of fiber.

name: Quinoa Stuffed Peppers
preparation: Cut bell pepper tops off; remove seeds.
Mix quinoa, black beans, corn, tomato, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Stuff peppers with the quinoa mixture.
Top with shredded cheese.
Bake at 190°C (375°F) for 25 minutes until peppers are tender.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Quinoa stuffed peppers with Tex-Mex flavors, complete meal.

name: Brown Rice Bowl
preparation: Place warm brown rice as the base in a bowl.
Season black beans with cumin and salt; warm in a pan.
Arrange black beans, avocado, corn, and tomatoes on the rice.
Squeeze lime juice over everything.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Add hot sauce if desired and serve.
notes: Hearty brown rice bowl with beans and avocado, balanced dinner.

name: Wild Rice Mushroom
preparation: Cook wild rice blend according to package instructions.
Heat butter; sauté onion and garlic until soft.
Add mushrooms and cook on high heat until golden, about 5 minutes.
Add dried thyme and vegetable stock; simmer for 3 minutes.
Add cooked wild rice and toss to combine.
Season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve.
notes: Earthy wild rice with sautéed mushrooms, elegant and satisfying.

name: Baked Sweet Potato
ingredients: 1 tbsp Greek yogurt or sour cream
preparation: Prick sweet potatoes with a fork; bake at 200°C for 45-50 minutes until soft.
Cut open and fluff the flesh with a fork.
Warm black beans with corn in a small pan.
Top each sweet potato with beans, corn, and salsa.
Add a dollop of Greek yogurt and diced avocado.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Loaded baked sweet potato, a nutritious and filling dinner.

name: Sweet Potato Black Bean Bowl
preparation: Toss sweet potato cubes with olive oil, cumin, chilli powder, and salt.
Roast at 200°C for 25 minutes until tender and caramelized.
Place brown rice in a bowl as the base.
Top with roasted sweet potato and black beans.
Add avocado slices and squeeze lime juice.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Sweet potato and black bean bowl, fiber and protein packed.

name: Loaded Baked Potato Light
preparation: Prick potatoes and bake at 200°C for 45-50 minutes until soft.
Cut open and fluff the inside with a fork.
Season with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Top with Greek yogurt, broccoli, and shredded cheese.
Garnish with chives.
Serve immediately while hot.
notes: Lighter loaded potato using Greek yogurt instead of sour cream.

name: Chicken Soup
preparation: Heat a pot; add a tsp of oil, sauté onion, garlic, carrot, and celery.
Add chicken pieces and cook until sealed on outside.
Add chicken stock, thyme, bay leaf, pepper, and salt.
Bring to a boil; reduce heat and simmer 20 minutes.
Remove bay leaf.
Garnish with fresh parsley.
Serve hot with crusty bread.
notes: Classic chicken soup, comforting and nourishing for dinner.

name: Vegetable Broth Bowl
preparation: Heat olive oil; sauté onion and garlic until fragrant.
Add carrot, celery, and zucchini; cook for 3 minutes.
Add vegetable stock, peas, herbs, and bay leaf.
Bring to a boil; simmer for 15 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Remove bay leaf.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve warm as a light dinner.
notes: Simple vegetable broth bowl, very low calorie and hydrating.

name: Bone Broth Bowl
preparation: Heat bone broth in a pot; add garlic, ginger, and turmeric.
Simmer for 5 minutes to infuse flavors.
Add carrot and mushrooms; cook for 5 minutes.
Add spinach/kale and stir until wilted.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve with a squeeze of lemon.
notes: Nutrient-rich bone broth bowl with vegetables, gut-healing dinner.

name: Turkey Burger Plate
preparation: Mix ground turkey with breadcrumbs, egg white, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper.
Shape into 2 patties.
Grill or pan-fry for 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through.
Toast the buns lightly.
Assemble burgers with lettuce, tomato, onion, and condiment.
Serve with a side salad.
notes: Lean turkey burger, a healthier alternative to beef burgers.

name: Veggie Burger
preparation: Mash black beans; mix with oats, onion, garlic, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Form into 2 patties; refrigerate for 15 minutes to firm up.
Heat oil in a pan; cook patties for 4-5 minutes per side.
Toast buns lightly.
Assemble with lettuce, tomato, and avocado.
Serve with a side of baked sweet potato fries.
notes: Homemade black bean veggie burger, high fiber and satisfying.

name: Portobello Burger
preparation: Remove stems from portobello caps; clean gills gently.
Mix olive oil, balsamic vinegar, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Brush mushroom caps with the marinade.
Grill or pan-sear for 4-5 minutes per side until tender.
Place cheese on top during last minute to melt (if using).
Assemble in buns with lettuce, tomato, and onion.
Serve immediately.
notes: Meaty portobello burger, a satisfying mushroom-based dinner.

name: Grilled Asparagus Plate
preparation: Trim woody ends from asparagus spears.
Toss with olive oil, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Grill on medium-high heat for 3-4 minutes, turning once.
Asparagus should be tender-crisp with char marks.
Squeeze lemon juice over the grilled asparagus.
Garnish with shaved parmesan if desired.
Serve as a dinner side or light plate.
notes: Grilled asparagus with lemon, elegant and low-calorie dinner side.

name: Roasted Broccoli Bowl
preparation: Toss broccoli with olive oil, garlic, chilli flakes, salt, and pepper.
Spread on a baking tray; roast at 200°C for 20-25 minutes until crispy edges.
Place quinoa in a bowl as the base.
Top with roasted broccoli.
Drizzle tahini and lemon juice over the bowl.
Serve warm.
notes: Crispy roasted broccoli bowl with tahini, nutrient-dense dinner.

name: Steamed Vegetable Platter
preparation: Arrange all vegetables in a steamer basket.
Steam over boiling water for 8-10 minutes until tender-crisp.
Transfer to a serving platter.
Drizzle with butter/olive oil and lemon juice.
Season with salt, pepper, and fresh herbs.
Serve as a light, clean dinner.
notes: Simple steamed vegetables, the lightest possible dinner option.

name: Kimchi Stew Light
preparation: Heat sesame oil; sauté onion and garlic briefly.
Add chopped kimchi and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add gochugaru and stir for 1 minute.
Pour in water/stock and soy sauce; bring to a boil.
Add tofu cubes and simmer for 10 minutes.
Adjust salt if needed.
Garnish with spring onion and serve with rice.
notes: Spicy Korean kimchi stew with tofu, probiotic-rich dinner.

name: Korean Bibimbap
preparation: Prepare each vegetable separately: blanch spinach, sauté carrot, mushrooms, and zucchini.
Blanch bean sprouts briefly.
Place warm rice in a bowl.
Arrange all vegetables neatly on top of the rice in sections.
Place fried egg in the center.
Add a dollop of gochujang and drizzle sesame oil.
Mix everything together before eating.
notes: Korean bibimbap, a colorful and balanced mixed rice bowl.

name: Korean BBQ Chicken Light
preparation: Mix gochujang, soy sauce, honey, sesame oil, garlic, and ginger.
Marinate chicken thighs for at least 1 hour.
Grill or pan-sear chicken for 5-6 minutes per side until cooked.
Let rest for 5 minutes; slice.
Serve sliced chicken with lettuce leaves for wrapping.
Garnish with sesame seeds.
Serve with steamed rice and pickled radish.
notes: Korean BBQ chicken with gochujang, wrapped in fresh lettuce.

name: Pesto Pasta Light
preparation: Cook pasta according to package instructions; reserve 1/4 cup pasta water.
Drain pasta and return to pot.
Add pesto and toss; add a splash of pasta water to loosen.
Add cherry tomatoes and olive oil; toss gently.
Plate and top with pine nuts and parmesan.
Garnish with fresh basil.
Serve immediately.
notes: Light pesto pasta with whole wheat, quick and flavorful dinner.

name: Lemon Pasta Light
preparation: Cook pasta al dente; reserve 1/2 cup pasta water.
Heat olive oil and butter; add garlic and sauté briefly.
Add lemon juice, lemon zest, salt, and pepper.
Add drained pasta; toss with pasta water to create a light sauce.
Add parmesan and toss until melted and coated.
Garnish with fresh parsley.
Serve immediately.
notes: Bright lemon pasta, simple Italian-inspired dinner.

name: Mushroom Pasta Light
preparation: Cook pasta al dente; reserve 1/4 cup pasta water.
Heat olive oil; sauté garlic for 30 seconds.
Add mushrooms and cook on high heat until golden, about 5 minutes.
Add thyme, cream/milk, salt, and pepper; simmer 2 minutes.
Add pasta and toss; add pasta water if needed.
Sprinkle parmesan and parsley.
Serve hot.
notes: Creamy mushroom pasta using less cream, satisfying dinner.

name: Fish Tacos
preparation: Season fish with cumin, paprika, garlic powder, and salt.
Heat oil; cook fish for 3-4 minutes per side until flaky.
Warm tortillas on a dry tawa.
Mix Greek yogurt with lime juice for a crema.
Assemble tacos: tortilla, cabbage, fish, salsa, and crema.
Top with fresh coriander.
Serve with lime wedges.
notes: Fresh fish tacos with lime crema, light and delicious dinner.

name: Shrimp Tacos
preparation: Season shrimp with chilli powder, cumin, garlic powder, and salt.
Heat oil; cook shrimp 2-3 minutes per side until pink.
Warm tortillas on a dry pan.
Assemble: tortilla, cabbage, shrimp, mango salsa, and avocado.
Squeeze lime juice over each taco.
Serve immediately.
notes: Zesty shrimp tacos with mango salsa, tropical dinner vibes.

name: Cauliflower Tacos
preparation: Toss cauliflower with olive oil, cumin, paprika, garlic powder, and salt.
Roast at 200°C for 25 minutes until golden and tender.
Warm tortillas on a dry pan.
Assemble: tortilla, cabbage, roasted cauliflower, salsa, and avocado.
Squeeze lime juice over each taco.
Serve immediately.
notes: Roasted cauliflower tacos, a plant-based taco night option.

name: Tomato Mozzarella Platter
preparation: Alternate slices of tomato and mozzarella on a plate.
Tuck fresh basil leaves between each layer.
Drizzle with extra virgin olive oil.
Add balsamic glaze in a zigzag pattern.
Season with salt and freshly cracked pepper.
Serve at room temperature with crusty bread.
notes: Classic Caprese salad, an elegant Italian light dinner.

name: Nicoise
preparation: Boil eggs for 10 minutes; cool, peel, and halve.
Blanch green beans in boiling water for 3 minutes; cool.
Boil potato until tender; slice.
Arrange mixed greens on a plate.
Artfully arrange tuna, eggs, beans, tomatoes, olives, and potato on top.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, and mustard for dressing.
Drizzle dressing over the salad and serve.
notes: French Nicoise salad, a balanced and elegant dinner plate.

name: Fattoush
ingredients: 1 small pita bread, toasted and broken into pieces
preparation: Toast pita bread until crispy; break into bite-sized pieces.
Combine lettuce, cucumber, tomato, radishes, mint, and parsley in a bowl.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, sumac, and salt for dressing.
Pour dressing over the salad and toss well.
Add toasted pita pieces on top.
Serve immediately to maintain crunch.
notes: Lebanese fattoush salad with crispy pita, fresh and tangy.

name: Chicken Piccata Light
preparation: Pound chicken breasts to even thickness; season with salt and pepper.
Dredge lightly in flour; shake off excess.
Heat olive oil in a pan; cook chicken 3-4 minutes per side. Remove.
In the same pan, add butter, lemon juice, and chicken stock.
Add capers and simmer for 2 minutes until slightly reduced.
Return chicken to the pan and spoon sauce over.
Garnish with parsley and serve.
notes: Italian chicken piccata, tangy lemon-caper sauce over tender chicken.

name: Veal Scaloppini Light
ingredients: 1/8 cup Marsala wine or chicken stock
preparation: Pound veal thin; season with salt and pepper, dredge in flour.
Heat olive oil; sear veal 2 minutes per side. Remove.
Add butter and mushrooms to the pan; cook until golden.
Add Marsala/stock; simmer for 2-3 minutes until sauce reduces.
Return veal to pan and coat with sauce.
Garnish with parsley.
Serve with a side of vegetables.
notes: Elegant veal scaloppini with mushroom sauce, special dinner dish.

name: Pork Tenderloin Light
preparation: Mix garlic powder, paprika, rosemary, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Rub spice mixture all over the pork tenderloin.
Heat olive oil in an oven-safe pan; sear pork on all sides.
Mix honey and mustard; brush over the pork.
Transfer to oven; roast at 190°C for 20-25 minutes until internal temp is 65°C.
Rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
Serve with roasted vegetables.
notes: Lean pork tenderloin with honey mustard glaze, tender and juicy.

name: Ceviche
ingredients: 200g fresh white fish (sea bass or sole), cubed small
1/8 cup lime juice (enough to cover fish)
preparation: Cut fish into small, even cubes.
Place in a bowl and pour lime juice over; ensure fish is covered.
Refrigerate for 30-45 minutes until fish turns opaque (citrus 'cooks' it).
Drain excess lime juice.
Add red onion, tomato, jalapeño, and coriander; mix gently.
Season with salt and pepper; add avocado.
Serve cold with tortilla chips.
notes: Fresh ceviche, citrus-marinated fish with Latin American flavors.

name: Hawaiian Poke Bowl
ingredients: Sesame seeds and nori strips for garnish
preparation: Cut sushi-grade fish into even cubes.
Toss fish with soy sauce, sesame oil, and rice vinegar.
Place sushi rice in a bowl as the base.
Arrange marinated fish, edamame, cucumber, and avocado on top.
Sprinkle sesame seeds and nori strips.
Serve immediately.
notes: Hawaiian poke bowl with fresh fish, a trendy and healthy dinner.

name: Sashimi Plate
ingredients: Shiso leaves or perilla for garnish
preparation: Ensure fish is sushi-grade fresh. Keep refrigerated until serving.
Slice salmon into thin, uniform pieces at an angle.
Slice tuna similarly into clean cuts.
Arrange slices neatly on a chilled plate with shiso leaves.
Place shredded daikon as a bed garnish.
Serve with small dishes of soy sauce, wasabi, and pickled ginger.
Enjoy fresh without any cooking.
notes: Pure sashimi plate, the freshest way to enjoy fish for dinner.

name: Ratatouille
preparation: Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F).
Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil until soft.
Spread crushed tomatoes in the bottom of a baking dish.
Arrange alternating slices of eggplant, zucchini, squash, and tomato upright in the dish.
Drizzle with olive oil; sprinkle herbs de Provence, salt, and pepper.
Cover with foil and bake for 45 minutes; uncover for last 15 minutes.
Garnish with fresh basil and serve.
notes: Classic French ratatouille, a beautiful and healthy vegetable dinner.

name: Air Fryer Chicken Kofta
preparation: Mix mince with all ingredients.
Shape into round kofta balls.
Spray with oil.
Air fry at 190°C for 14-16 minutes, shaking halfway.
Serve with gravy or as dry starter.
notes: Light dinner koftas.

name: Air Fryer Paneer Steak
preparation: Marinate paneer in curd and spices.
Air fry at 190°C for 10-12 minutes, flipping once.
Serve with sautéed veggies and salad.
notes: High-protein vegetarian steak.

name: Air Fryer Stuffed Mushrooms
preparation: Mix paneer, capsicum, coriander and spices.
Stuff mushroom caps with mixture.
Spray with oil.
Air fry at 180°C for 10-12 minutes.
Serve as appetizer or light dinner.
notes: Elegant low-carb dinner.

name: Air Fryer Fish Tikka
preparation: Marinate fish in curd and spices for 30 min.
Place in air fryer basket.
Air fry at 200°C for 10-12 minutes.
Serve with mint chutney and onion salad.
notes: Tender, spiced fish tikka.

name: Air Fryer Aloo Gobi
preparation: Toss cauliflower and potato with oil and spices.
Air fry at 190°C for 18-20 minutes, shaking every 6 min.
Serve with roti and dal.
notes: Classic aloo gobi, minimal oil.

name: Air Fryer Lamb Chops
preparation: Marinate lamb chops in curd and spices for 2 hours.
Spray basket, place chops in single layer.
Air fry at 200°C for 18-20 minutes, flipping once.
Rest 5 minutes.
Serve with mint chutney and salad.
notes: Tender, juicy lamb chops.

name: Air Fryer Tofu Tikka
preparation: Press tofu for 15 min, cube.
Marinate tofu and veggies in curd and spices.
Air fry at 190°C for 14 minutes, turning once.
Serve with roti and chutney.
notes: Vegan-friendly protein-rich tikka.

name: Air Fryer Keema Stuffed Paratha
preparation: Cook keema with onion, ginger-garlic, garam masala until dry.
Roll dough, stuff with keema, seal and re-roll.
Spray and air fry at 180°C for 5 min per side.
Serve with raita.
notes: Hearty stuffed paratha for dinner.

name: Air Fryer Vegetable Spring Rolls
preparation: Stir-fry vegetables briefly with soy sauce, ginger and pepper.
Cool the filling.
Place filling on wrappers, fold and roll tightly.
Spray with oil.
Air fry at 190°C for 10-12 minutes until golden.
Serve with sweet chilli sauce.
notes: Crispy rolls without deep frying.

name: Air Fryer Chicken Shawarma
preparation: Marinate chicken in curd and spices for 1 hour.
Air fry at 200°C for 14 minutes.
Warm pita in air fryer for 1 min.
Fill pita with chicken, lettuce and tomato.
Drizzle with garlic sauce.
notes: Middle Eastern street food at home.

name: Air Fryer Baingan Bharta Patties
preparation: Air fry whole brinjal at 200°C for 20 min until charred.
Peel and mash the flesh.
Mix with onion, tomato, chilli, cumin, besan and salt.
Shape into patties.
Air fry at 180°C for 10-12 min, flipping once.
Serve with roti.
notes: Smoky baingan bharta in patty form.

name: Air Fryer Egg Fried Rice
preparation: Toss rice with oil, soy sauce and pepper.
Place in air fryer-safe pan.
Pour beaten egg over rice.
Air fry at 180°C for 10 minutes, stirring halfway.
Add vegetables, air fry 5 more minutes.
Garnish with spring onion.
notes: Quick fried rice without the wok.

name: Air Fryer Hara Bhara Kebab
preparation: Blend spinach and peas coarsely.
Mix with mashed potato, spices and cornflour.
Shape into round kebabs.
Spray with oil.
Air fry at 180°C for 12-14 minutes.
Serve with green chutney.
notes: Green, iron-rich vegetarian kebabs.

name: Air Fryer Chicken Tikka Masala Bowl
preparation: Marinate chicken in curd and tikka masala.
Air fry at 200°C for 14 minutes.
Serve over rice, drizzle masala sauce.
Garnish with coriander.
notes: Complete dinner bowl.

name: Air Fryer Stuffed Aloo Paratha
preparation: Mix potato with all spices.
Roll dough, stuff with potato, seal and re-roll.
Spray both sides.
Air fry at 180°C for 5 minutes per side.
Serve with pickle and curd.
notes: Classic aloo paratha, less oil.

name: Air Fryer Pepper Chicken
preparation: Marinate chicken with pepper, ginger-garlic, turmeric and salt.
Air fry at 200°C for 14-16 minutes.
Add onion and curry leaves, cook 3 more minutes.
Serve hot.
notes: Spicy South Indian pepper chicken.

name: Air Fryer Gobi Manchurian
preparation: Coat florets in cornflour and maida batter.
Air fry at 190°C for 14 minutes until crispy.
Toss with soy sauce, chilli sauce and ginger-garlic.
Garnish with spring onion.
Serve as dry starter or with fried rice.
notes: Indo-Chinese favourite, healthier version.

name: Air Fryer Paneer Pakora
preparation: Mix besan, rice flour and spices with a little water.
Dip paneer slices in batter.
Spray and air fry at 190°C for 10-12 min, flipping once.
Serve with green chutney.
notes: Crispy paneer pakora with minimal oil.

name: Air Fryer Chicken Momos
preparation: Mix mince with onion, soy sauce, ginger, garlic, salt and pepper.
Fill wrappers with mixture, shape into momos.
Spray basket, place momos.
Air fry at 180°C for 12-14 minutes.
Serve with spicy red chutney.
notes: Steamed-meets-crispy momos.

name: Air Fryer Achari Paneer
preparation: Mix curd, achari masala and mustard oil.
Marinate paneer for 30 min.
Air fry at 190°C for 10-12 minutes.
Serve with roomali roti.
notes: Tangy, spicy pickle-flavoured paneer.

name: Grilled Chicken Kofta
preparation: Mix mince with all ingredients.
Shape onto skewers.
Grill on high for 5-6 min per side.
Serve with naan and raita.
notes: Smoky grilled koftas.

name: Grilled Paneer Steak with Veggies
preparation: Season paneer with pepper, garlic and salt.
Toss veggies with oil and salt.
Grill paneer and veggies for 4-5 min per side.
Serve together.
notes: Complete grilled dinner plate.

name: Grilled Fish Tikka
preparation: Marinate fish for 30 min.
Thread onto skewers.
Grill on medium for 4-5 min per side.
Serve with mint chutney.
notes: Tender grilled fish tikka.

name: Grilled Stuffed Mushrooms
preparation: Mix paneer, capsicum, coriander and spices.
Stuff mushroom caps.
Grill on medium for 8-10 min until tender.
Serve as starter or light dinner.
notes: Elegant low-carb dinner.

name: Grilled Chicken Breast with Herbs
preparation: Marinate chicken with herbs, garlic, pepper, lemon and oil.
Grill on medium-high for 6-7 min per side.
Rest 5 min.
Slice and serve with salad.
notes: Simple herb-grilled chicken.

name: Grilled Baingan (Eggplant)
preparation: Score eggplant slices, rub with oil and spices.
Grill on medium for 5-6 min per side.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve with roti.
notes: Smoky, spiced eggplant.

name: Grilled Tandoori Prawns
preparation: Marinate prawns for 30 min.
Thread onto skewers.
Grill on high for 2-3 min per side.
Serve with lemon wedges.
notes: Quick tandoori prawns.

name: Grilled Soya Tikka
preparation: Marinate soya and veggies in curd and spices.
Thread onto skewers.
Grill for 5-6 min per side.
Serve with mint chutney.
notes: High-protein vegetarian tikka.

name: Grilled Lamb Seekh Kebab
preparation: Mix mince with all ingredients.
Shape onto skewers.
Grill on high for 5-6 min per side.
Serve with roomali roti.
notes: Rich, flavourful lamb kebabs.

name: Grilled Aloo Tikki
preparation: Mix potato, peas, spices and cornflour.
Shape into patties.
Brush with oil and grill for 5-6 min per side.
Serve with curd and chutney.
notes: Crispy grilled tikkis.

name: Grilled Chicken Shawarma Plate
preparation: Marinate chicken in curd and spices for 1 hour.
Grill on high for 5-6 min per side.
Slice and serve over rice with salad.
notes: Complete shawarma dinner plate.

name: Grilled Vegetable Wrap
preparation: Grill vegetables with oil for 4 min per side.
Warm tortilla.
Spread hummus, add grilled veggies.
Roll and serve.
notes: Healthy grilled veggie wrap.

name: Grilled Hariyali Chicken
preparation: Blend chutney with curd and ginger-garlic.
Marinate chicken for 2 hours.
Grill on skewers for 6 min per side.
Serve with naan.
notes: Vibrant green chicken tikka.

name: Grilled Paneer Tikka Wrap
preparation: Marinate and grill paneer and veggies.
Warm tortilla, spread mint chutney.
Fill with grilled paneer and veggies.
Roll tightly and serve.
notes: Protein-packed paneer wrap.

name: Grilled Pomfret
preparation: Score fish on both sides.
Apply spice paste and lemon, marinate 30 min.
Wrap in banana leaf or place directly on grill.
Grill for 7-8 min per side.
Serve with rice.
notes: Traditional grilled pomfret.
